title: Surface bridge counters + producing-state on the CapturingStream trait
description:
Prerequisite for stream_stats() and windowed backpressure: expose counters BridgeShared already maintains (ring_buffer.rs:119-123 buffers_pushed/buffers_dropped/buffers_popped) through the public CapturingStream trait (src/core/interface.rs:91). Add default-implemented accessors so every existing impl and the mocks (api.rs:1267, stream.rs) keep compiling: fn buffers_captured(&self)->u64{0} (maps to buffers_popped, delivered-to-consumer), fn buffers_dropped(&self)->u64{0} (maps to overrun_count), fn buffers_pushed(&self)->u64{0}. Override on BridgeStream<S> (stream.rs:180 impl) reading self.shared atomics with Ordering::Relaxed, mirroring overrun_count() at stream.rs:271. Keep overrun_count() as its existing alias. Do NOT add windowing here.

Acceptance criteria:
- CapturingStream gains buffers_captured(), buffers_pushed(), buffers_dropped() with default-0 bodies; MinimalStream test (stream.rs:651) and all impls compile unchanged
- BridgeStream overrides read the three BridgeShared atomics with Relaxed ordering
- Unit test asserts buffers_pushed==successful pushes, buffers_dropped==dropped pushes, buffers_captured==popped count after a scripted push/drop/pop sequence
- cargo test passes with default features

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 1)

title: Add start_instant uptime tracking to AudioCapture
description:
Add start_instant: Option<std::time::Instant> to AudioCapture (src/api.rs:432). Set to Some(Instant::now()) exactly once, the first time start() actually creates the stream (api.rs:450, in the self.stream.is_none() branch after the stream is built); leave untouched on idempotent restart. Clear to None in stop() (api.rs:588) and Drop (api.rs:938) when the stream is torn down. Add private fn uptime(&self)->Option<Duration> = self.start_instant.map(|t| t.elapsed()). Update both make_mock_capture (api.rs:1318) and the no-stream test constructor (api.rs:1414) to initialize the field. Single Instant store on the non-RT control path.

Acceptance criteria:
- AudioCapture has start_instant: Option<Instant>; all struct literals in api.rs (build(), make_mock_capture, overrun_count_returns_zero_when_no_stream) updated
- start() sets start_instant only on first real stream creation; a second start() on a running stream does not reset it
- stop() and Drop reset start_instant to None alongside dropping the stream Arc
- Test: after start(), uptime() is Some and monotonically non-decreasing across two reads; after stop(), uptime() is None
- cargo test passes

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 1)

title: Add AudioCapture::format() exposing the negotiated delivery format
description:
The bridge already computes and atomically publishes the authoritative delivery format (BridgeShared::negotiated_format at ring_buffer.rs:197, surfaced via BridgeStream::format at stream.rs:258), but there is no public read path from the AudioCapture handle (only overrun_count is exposed, api.rs:871). Add impl AudioCapture { pub fn format(&self)->Option<AudioFormat> } returning self.stream.as_ref().map(|s| s.format()); None before start(). Add a private fn format_description_string(fmt:&AudioFormat)->String producing e.g. '2ch 48000Hz F32' for stream_stats() to use lazily on the query path (not stored per-buffer). Single Acquire load behind the existing trait method.

Acceptance criteria:
- AudioCapture::format() returns Some(negotiated AudioFormat) once started, None before start()
- Test (mock): a stream whose backend called set_negotiated_format(44100,1) makes AudioCapture::format() report sample_rate 44100, channels 1, normalized F32 (mirrors test_format_reflects_negotiated_delivery_format in stream.rs)
- format_description_string yields a non-empty 'Nch RHz FMT' string used by stream_stats()
- No new allocation unless format()/stream_stats() is actually called
- cargo test passes

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 1)

title: Implement AudioCapture::stream_stats() and complete the StreamStats struct (H3)
description:
Honor the broken doc contract at introspection.rs:326 ('Obtained via AudioCapture::stream_stats()' — method absent today; verified StreamStats has only overruns/is_running/format_description and no #[non_exhaustive]). (1) Mark StreamStats (introspection.rs:327) #[non_exhaustive] and add fields buffers_captured:u64, buffers_dropped:u64, buffers_pushed:u64, uptime:std::time::Duration; keep existing overruns/is_running/format_description. Add impl StreamStats { pub fn dropped_ratio(&self)->f64 } = dropped/(captured+dropped) (or /pushed) with a zero-denominator guard returning 0.0. (2) Add impl AudioCapture { pub fn stream_stats(&self)->StreamStats } that bundle-reads the trait accessors (buffers_captured/dropped/pushed/overrun_count), is_running from self.is_running(), format_description via format_description_string from format(), uptime from self.uptime().unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO). No stream => StreamStats::default() with is_running=false. Construct field-by-field internally (allowed in the defining crate). Update the doc comment so the intra-doc link resolves under deny(broken_intra_doc_links).

Acceptance criteria:
- StreamStats is #[non_exhaustive] and gains buffers_captured/buffers_dropped/buffers_pushed/uptime plus dropped_ratio() with divide-by-zero guard (0.0 when denom==0; 0.25 for captured=3 dropped=1)
- AudioCapture::stream_stats() exists and is re-exported; the introspection.rs doc comment no longer references a non-existent method and rustdoc intra-doc link resolves
- Test (mock): after pushing N, dropping M, popping K, stream_stats() reports buffers_dropped==M, buffers_captured==K, is_running==true, non-decreasing uptime across two calls
- Test: stream_stats() on a never-started capture returns is_running=false, uptime==ZERO, no panic
- cargo build clean under -D warnings; cargo test passes

Depends on: Surface bridge counters + producing-state on the CapturingStream trait; Add start_instant uptime tracking to AudioCapture; Add AudioCapture::format() exposing the negotiated delivery format

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Implement CaptureTarget FromStr + TryFrom<&str> + Display round-trip (H1)
description:
Verified absent: config.rs:54 enum has no Display/FromStr (only DeviceId/ApplicationId/ProcessId have Display). Add impl std::str::FromStr for CaptureTarget { type Err = AudioError; ... }, impl TryFrom<&str> (delegating to FromStr), and impl std::fmt::Display in src/core/config.rs. Grammar (case-insensitive scheme prefix, round-trips with Display): system/default -> SystemDefault; device:<id> -> Device(DeviceId(id)) (colon in id preserved after first split); app:<body> -> Application(ApplicationId(body)) when body parses as integer-or-string per existing convention, name:<s> -> ApplicationByName(s); pid:<n>/tree:<n> -> ProcessTree(ProcessId(n)). Display emits the canonical form FromStr round-trips (ProcessTree(7)->'tree:7', ApplicationByName('Firefox')->'name:Firefox'). Errors return AudioError::InvalidParameter{param:'capture_target', reason} for unknown scheme or unparseable pid. Use exhaustive match in Display so a new variant forces a compile error. (Note: chose AudioError::InvalidParameter — matches existing api.rs error style; bindings map from it. Convenience constructors CaptureTarget::app/pid/device already exist at introspection.rs:85-101 and complement this.)

Acceptance criteria:
- 'system'/'default' parse to SystemDefault; 'device:hw:0,0' preserves the colon in id; 'app:Spotify' and 'name:VLC' parse to the right variants; 'tree:99' and 'pid:99' both -> ProcessTree(ProcessId(99))
- Round-trip: for every variant, CaptureTarget::from_str(&t.to_string())==Ok(t.clone())
- Unknown scheme ('bogus:x') and non-numeric pid ('pid:abc') return AudioError::InvalidParameter with param=='capture_target', never panic
- TryFrom<&str> yields identical results to FromStr
- cargo test --lib passes new unit tests covering each variant + round-trip + error cases

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 1)

title: Fix rsac-python feature hygiene: target-conditional backends instead of hardcoded feat_macos
description:
bindings/rsac-python/Cargo.toml (lines 19-22) sets default-features=false then features=['feat_macos'], so the crate fails to compile on Windows/Linux (no backend). Replace with target-conditional features: [target.'cfg(windows)'.dependencies.rsac] features=['feat_windows']; [target.'cfg(target_os = "linux")'...] feat_linux; [target.'cfg(target_os = "macos")'...] feat_macos — each with default-features=false, path='../..'. Update pyproject.toml [tool.maturin] only if a feature passthrough is needed. This is the single biggest packaging correctness bug.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo build -p rsac-python succeeds on each of Windows, Linux, macOS with the correct single backend compiled
- No feat_macos hardcode remains in rsac-python/Cargo.toml
- maturin build (wheel) succeeds on the host platform

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 1)

title: Add criterion benchmark harness + benches/ scaffolding for the bridge data plane
description:
Verified: no benches/ dir exists; criterion 0.8.2 already a dev-dependency (Cargo.toml:133). Create benches/ and wire [[bench]] name='bridge' harness=false into Cargo.toml. Add benches/bridge.rs measuring the current hot path: (1) throughput samples/sec via create_bridge + BridgeProducer::push_samples_or_drop (ring_buffer.rs:305) with a 1024-frame stereo slice; (2) push->pop round-trip latency via BridgeConsumer::pop (ring_buffer.rs:452); (3) capacity sensitivity sweep over {16,32,64,128,256} from calculate_capacity (ring_buffer.rs:653). Use criterion::black_box and Throughput::Elements. This is the baseline ALL later perf tasks compare against. No production code changes.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo bench --bench bridge builds and runs on the default feature set with no platform backend required
- benches/bridge.rs covers push throughput, push->pop round-trip, and a capacity sweep over at least {16,64,256}
- Cargo.toml declares the bench with harness=false; it does not affect cargo build/cargo test
- A baseline result is captured in the criterion target dir and the bench uses black_box to defeat dead-code elimination

(effort: medium | verify: bench | wave: 1)

title: Add an allocation-counting test harness asserting push_samples_or_drop is alloc-free in steady state
description:
ADR-0001 (docs/designs/0001-rt-allocation-guarantee.md) claims steady-state alloc-free but nothing measures it. Add a #[cfg(test)] CountingAllocator(System) wrapper with an AtomicUsize bump in alloc/realloc/dealloc, exposed via with_alloc_count(|| {...})->usize in tests/. Add tests/rt_alloc.rs (its own integration binary, run single-threaded because #[global_allocator] is process-wide): warm up BridgeProducer::push_samples_or_drop (ring_buffer.rs:305) + BridgeConsumer::pop for N iterations to populate the free-list, then assert ZERO heap allocations across a steady-state push/pop loop at a fixed 1024-frame stereo period. Also assert a period INCREASE triggers at most a bounded one-time allocation (ADR-0001 'bounded one-time growth'). Counting allocator must never reach shipped library code.

Acceptance criteria:
- A counting allocator instruments alloc/realloc/dealloc, usable only in test scope
- tests/rt_alloc.rs warms the free-list then asserts 0 allocations across >=1000 steady-state push_samples_or_drop+pop cycles at constant period
- Test asserts a period increase triggers at most a bounded one-time allocation
- Test runs single-threaded in its own integration binary and passes on the host toolchain

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 1)

title: Add zero-alloc AudioBuffer metering: rms/peak/dBFS, channel-strided, NaN-safe (H2)
description:
Single source of truth replacing hand-rolled meters (rms_level in src/main.rs:480/296-302, calculate_rms in src/bin/pipewire_test.rs:309, and the rsac-python reimplementation at rsac-python/src/lib.rs:335-353). Add to AudioBuffer (src/core/buffer.rs, after channel_data ~line 248-262), all #[inline]: pub fn rms(&self)->f32 (sqrt(mean(x^2)) over self.data; 0.0 if empty), peak(&self)->f32 (max|x|; 0.0 if empty), rms_dbfs/peak_dbfs (NEG_INFINITY at silence, 20*log10(level) otherwise), channel_rms(ch:u16)->Option<f32> / channel_peak via strided self.data.iter().skip(ch).step_by(channels) with NO Vec::collect (unlike channel_data). Skip non-finite samples (is_finite filter) so NaN/Inf source (tolerated by buffer_with_nan_and_infinity at buffer.rs:651) cannot poison the meter. Add private fn lin_to_dbfs(level:f32)->f32. Then refactor src/main.rs and src/bin/pipewire_test.rs to call the new methods and delete the local helpers.

Acceptance criteria:
- rms()/peak() of [0.5,-0.5,0.5,-0.5]==0.5; empty buffer returns 0.0 (no NaN/panic)
- rms_dbfs() of silence == NEG_INFINITY; peak_dbfs() of full-scale 1.0 ~ 0.0 dB within 1e-4
- channel_rms(0)/channel_peak(0) on stereo [L,R,L,R] match the deinterleaved channel; out-of-range or channel==channels returns None; no .collect()/.to_vec() in their bodies (inspection)
- A buffer with NaN/Inf yields finite rms()/peak(), no panic
- src/main.rs rms_level and src/bin/pipewire_test.rs calculate_rms are deleted and call sites use the new methods; CLI still builds and runs; cargo test passes

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 1)

title: Add RT-safe windowed drop-rate tracking and BackpressureReport (M6)
description:
Replace the all-or-nothing consecutive-drop signal (is_under_backpressure() bool at api.rs:881 / ring_buffer.rs:158, which resets on ANY successful push and misses 1-in-3 sustained loss patterns) with a windowed view. Add to BridgeShared (ring_buffer.rs:115) a fixed-size alloc-free ring of recent per-window snapshots: a [AtomicU64; N] of packed (pushed,dropped) plus an AtomicU64 window cursor/epoch — NO Mutex, NO allocation — updated with Relaxed adds on the producer's existing push/push_or_drop/push_samples_or_drop paths (ring_buffer.rs:249/269/322/349). Define #[non_exhaustive] pub struct BackpressureReport { pub window: Duration, pub pushed: u64, pub dropped: u64, pub drop_rate: f64, pub is_under_backpressure: bool } (in src/core/introspection.rs alongside StreamStats, or a new observability.rs), re-exported from lib.rs, with drop_rate computed read-side and a zero-division guard. Add CapturingStream::backpressure_report(&self)->BackpressureReport with an all-zero default, override on BridgeStream bundle-reading the atomics, and AudioCapture::backpressure_report(&self) returning zeroed when no stream. The legacy bool stays unchanged and is carried inside the report. This is the single canonical windowed implementation (the Ergonomics-M6 deferred-windowing snapshot-only variant is dropped in favor of this).

Acceptance criteria:
- BackpressureReport is #[non_exhaustive], defined once, re-exported from lib.rs; drop_rate==0.0 when pushed+dropped==0
- Windowing uses only fixed preallocated atomics — no Vec/Mutex/Box on push/push_or_drop/push_samples_or_drop (PR inspection note confirms)
- CapturingStream gains backpressure_report() (all-zero default); AudioCapture::backpressure_report() and the BridgeStream override exist
- Test: a drop,push,drop,push pattern that NEVER trips the consecutive-threshold bool still reports drop_rate ~0.5 in the windowed report
- Legacy is_under_backpressure() behavior unchanged (default_backpressure_threshold_applied / custom_backpressure_threshold_trips_and_resets still pass); cargo test passes

Depends on: Surface bridge counters + producing-state on the CapturingStream trait

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Add AudioError::user_message() -> UserFacingError with remedy hints (M5, exhaustive)
description:
Turn the error taxonomy into actionable UI text without changing it. In src/core/error.rs add #[non_exhaustive] pub struct UserFacingError { pub summary: String, pub remedy: Option<String>, pub recoverability: Recoverability, pub kind: ErrorKind } and impl AudioError { pub fn user_message(&self)->UserFacingError } using an EXHAUSTIVE match over all 22 variants (no _ arm, mirroring recoverability() at error.rs:245) so a new variant forces a compile error. Each arm: plain-language summary + remedy where actionable (PermissionDenied -> 'Grant audio capture permission in System Settings > Privacy (macOS 14.4+)'; PlatformNotSupported -> name the platform/feature + 'check PlatformCapabilities::query()'; DeviceNotFound -> 'List devices with list_audio_sources()'; StreamEnded -> 'create a new capture'). Populate recoverability/kind from self.recoverability() (error.rs:245) and self.kind() (error.rs:203). Re-export UserFacingError from lib.rs and prelude. Error path only.

Acceptance criteria:
- user_message() compiles with an exhaustive match (no _ catch-all; deleting a variant arm causes a compile error)
- Test constructs all 22 variants (reuse make_all_variants at error.rs:499) and asserts every summary is non-empty
- PermissionDenied and PlatformNotSupported arms include a non-empty remedy mentioning the action/platform
- user_message().recoverability and .kind equal self.recoverability() and self.kind() for a representative sample
- UserFacingError is #[non_exhaustive] and re-exported from rsac root and rsac::prelude; cargo test passes

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Eliminate the consumer-side double-copy in BridgeConsumer::pop
description:
BridgeConsumer::pop (ring_buffer.rs:452-478) does let user_copy = original.clone() — a full Vec<f32> alloc + memcpy on every pop — then recycles original to the producer. Instead, give the user the buffer that travelled the ring directly (move, no clone) and recycle a spare Vec pulled from a small consumer-side free pool (or Vec::new() when empty — allocation stays off the RT thread, the constraint that matters). Internal change only; pop() still returns an owned AudioBuffer. Net: one fewer alloc+copy per delivered buffer. Verify against the bridge benchmark and the alloc-probe (producer path must stay 0-alloc; consumer alloc count must drop vs baseline).

Acceptance criteria:
- BridgeConsumer::pop no longer calls .clone() on the ring's Vec<f32>; the delivered buffer is moved
- The producer free-list (free_rx) is still replenished so push_samples_or_drop remains 0-alloc in steady state (alloc-probe still passes)
- FIFO order, timestamp, and format preservation tests (push_samples_then_pop_preserves_data, pop_preserves_timestamp_through_recycle) still pass
- The push->pop round-trip benchmark shows a measurable reduction in per-buffer time/allocs vs the captured baseline

Depends on: Add criterion benchmark harness + benches/ scaffolding for the bridge data plane; Add an allocation-counting test harness asserting push_samples_or_drop is alloc-free in steady state

(effort: medium | verify: bench | wave: 2)

title: Add a sample-domain SPSC ring with rtrb 0.3.4 write_chunk_uninit (feature bridge-zerocopy, default off)
description:
rtrb write_chunk_uninit(n) + WriteChunkUninit/CopyToUninit write f32 directly into MaybeUninit slots, but only when the ring element type is f32 — the current bridge ring stores AudioBuffer (Vec-backed), so the Vec intermediate can't be removed without a sample-domain ring. Bump rtrb 0.3.3->0.3.4 in Cargo.toml first (REFERENCE_ANALYSIS confirms purely additive, no breaking changes; verified 0.3.3 pinned today). Add an internal SampleRing carrying interleaved f32 + a small parallel metadata channel (channels/rate/timestamp per chunk), behind a feature flag bridge-zerocopy (default OFF). push_samples_or_drop on the SampleRing writes via write_chunk_uninit + copy_to_uninit then commit — no Vec, no AudioBuffer alloc on the RT thread. Keep the existing AudioBuffer ring as the default path; A/B them in the benchmark. Public BridgeProducer/BridgeConsumer surface unchanged.

Acceptance criteria:
- rtrb bumped to 0.3.4 in Cargo.toml and Cargo.lock; existing tests pass
- Feature-gated SampleRing uses write_chunk_uninit + CopyToUninit with no Vec<f32> or AudioBuffer allocation on the producer call
- Frame metadata (channels, sample_rate, timestamp) is preserved so the consumer reconstructs equivalent AudioBuffers (parallels push_samples_then_pop_preserves_data)
- Criterion A/B compares the AudioBuffer ring vs the SampleRing on push throughput; the alloc-probe confirms the SampleRing producer path is literally 0-alloc post-warmup

Depends on: Add criterion benchmark harness + benches/ scaffolding for the bridge data plane; Add an allocation-counting test harness asserting push_samples_or_drop is alloc-free in steady state

(effort: large | verify: bench | wave: 2)

title: Add rsac::prelude module with explicit re-exports
description:
Add pub mod prelude; to src/lib.rs and create src/prelude.rs explicitly re-exporting the common capture surface so downstreams write use rsac::prelude::*; instead of the ~15 flat names (verified lib.rs:99-138). Re-export: AudioBuffer, AudioCapture, AudioCaptureBuilder, CaptureTarget, AudioFormat, SampleFormat, AudioError, AudioResult, ErrorKind, Recoverability, PlatformCapabilities, AudioSource, AudioSourceKind, StreamStats, AudioDevice, DeviceKind, the AudioSink/ChannelSink/NullSink sink types, plus #[cfg(feature='sink-wav')] WavFileSink and #[cfg(feature='async-stream')] AsyncAudioStream. Keep existing top-level re-exports unchanged (additive). Explicit re-exports (not a glob macro). Document each export group with a short module-doc.

Acceptance criteria:
- use rsac::prelude::*; compiles in a doctest and brings AudioCaptureBuilder/CaptureTarget/AudioBuffer/AudioError into scope
- prelude re-exports are feature-gated identically to lib.rs (sink-wav, async-stream) so --no-default-features still compiles the prelude
- No name is exported from prelude that is not already public at crate root
- A doctest in prelude.rs builds a SystemDefault capture using only prelude imports

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Add AudioCaptureBuilder::target_str() string-target setter
description:
Add pub fn target_str(self, s:&str)->AudioResult<Self> (and an infallible try variant) to AudioCaptureBuilder in src/api.rs (alongside with_target at api.rs:91). It parses s via CaptureTarget::from_str and calls self.with_target(target), surfacing the parse error. Lets CLI/config feed a raw string ('app:1234','device:hw:0,0') straight into the builder without hand-rolling the match. Keep with_target unchanged (additive).

Acceptance criteria:
- AudioCaptureBuilder::new().target_str('system')? sets target == SystemDefault (assert via build with a mock or a test accessor)
- target_str('app:55') sets Application(ApplicationId('55')); target_str('name:VLC') sets ApplicationByName('VLC')
- target_str('garbage') returns Err(AudioError::InvalidParameter) and does not mutate the builder's target
- Method is chainable (returns AudioResult<Self>) and documented with an example

Depends on: Implement CaptureTarget FromStr + TryFrom<&str> + Display round-trip (H1)

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: Add one-call AudioCaptureBuilder::start() returning a RunningCapture RAII guard
description:
Add pub fn start(self)->AudioResult<RunningCapture> to AudioCaptureBuilder (src/api.rs) calling self.build()? then capture.start()? and wrapping. Define pub struct RunningCapture(AudioCapture) with impl Deref<Target=AudioCapture> + DerefMut so all read/subscribe/stats methods are reachable, plus impl Drop { fn drop(&mut self){ let _ = self.0.stop(); } } for deterministic teardown (stop is idempotent and Drop-safe per api.rs:938). Add pub fn into_inner(self)->AudioCapture. Removes the build()-then-start() two-step. Keep build()/AudioCapture::start() public/unchanged. Implement via plain Deref/Drop — NOT ManuallyDrop (flagged as over-engineering).

Acceptance criteria:
- AudioCaptureBuilder::new().with_target(SystemDefault).start() returns AudioResult<RunningCapture> (compiles; runs against a mock-backed build path in test)
- RunningCapture derefs to AudioCapture: guard.read_buffer(), guard.stream_stats(), guard.is_running() callable through Deref/DerefMut
- Dropping a RunningCapture calls stop() exactly once (assert via a stream whose stop sets a flag/counter)
- into_inner() returns the wrapped AudioCapture without triggering the guard's stop()
- No double-stop: explicit stop() followed by drop does not error

Depends on: Implement AudioCapture::stream_stats() and complete the StreamStats struct (H3)

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: Add AudioCaptureBuilder::preflight() capability/format check before device resolution
description:
Add pub fn preflight(&self)->AudioResult<()> to AudioCaptureBuilder (src/api.rs) running the cheap device-independent validations currently embedded in build() (api.rs:136-182) WITHOUT opening a device: query PlatformCapabilities::query(), reject Application/ApplicationByName when !supports_application_capture and ProcessTree when !supports_process_tree_capture (same errors build() raises at api.rs:142/148), validate sample_rate against the supported set and channels in 1..=32. Refactor build() to call self.preflight()? first so the logic is single-sourced (no behavior change). Config-time only, no RT impact.

Acceptance criteria:
- preflight() returns Ok(()) for a valid SystemDefault/48000/2ch config
- preflight() returns AudioError::InvalidParameter{param:'sample_rate'} for 11025 without enumerating devices
- preflight() returns AudioError::ConfigurationError for channels==0 and channels>32
- On a platform where supports_application_capture is false, preflight() with Application returns PlatformNotSupported (same as build())
- Existing builder_fails_* tests pass unchanged, proving the refactor is behavior-preserving

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: Add synchronous per-call overflow reporting to the producer push API
description:
Overflow is only knowable post-hoc by polling buffers_dropped (ring_buffer.rs:386). Add an additive push_samples_reporting(&mut self, data, channels, sample_rate)->PushOutcome where PushOutcome { pushed: bool, dropped_this_call: u32 } (or reuse rtrb push_partial_slice's (pushed,remainder) pattern on the SampleRing). Surfaces overflow eagerly in the callback for cheap per-period drop-rate computation without extra atomic loads. Keep push_samples_or_drop (ring_buffer.rs:305) as the unchanged primary entry; the new method is additive and delegates. No allocation, no lock.

Acceptance criteria:
- push_samples_reporting returns whether the push succeeded AND how much was dropped in THIS call, without polling shared counters
- Existing push_samples_or_drop behavior and signature are unchanged
- The method performs no heap allocation and no locking (covered by the alloc-probe harness)
- Unit test asserts dropped-this-call reporting matches the delta in buffers_dropped across a full-ring scenario

Depends on: Add an allocation-counting test harness asserting push_samples_or_drop is alloc-free in steady state

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Add per-buffer stream-relative timestamps (push_samples_or_drop_at)
description:
Backends produce buffers via push_samples_or_drop (windows/thread.rs:517, macos/thread.rs:284, linux/thread.rs:1118), which always yields timestamp:None (AudioBuffer::new), making buffer-level latency/jitter unobservable even though AudioBuffer has a timestamp slot (buffer.rs:55) and the bridge preserves it through recycle (ring_buffer.rs:465, pop_preserves_timestamp_through_recycle). Add an overload BridgeProducer::push_samples_or_drop_at(&mut self, data, channels, sample_rate, timestamp: Duration)->bool constructing the buffer with AudioBuffer::with_timestamp (buffer.rs:115) instead of new, reusing the exact same free-list/scratch alloc-free path (refactor push_samples_or_drop to delegate with None). The timestamp is a cheap Instant-delta the backend computes against a per-stream start Instant it already holds — no extra clock syscall on the hot path beyond the cached elapsed(). Wire each backend to pass elapsed-since-stream-start. Metadata only; no audio transform.

Acceptance criteria:
- push_samples_or_drop_at exists and is alloc-free in steady state (delegates through the same free_rx/scratch logic; push_samples_or_drop delegates with None)
- A bridge test pushing via the timestamped variant and popping observes the timestamp survive the recycle round-trip
- At least one backend buildable in the verify tier passes a monotonically non-decreasing stream-relative timestamp; buffers read back carry Some(timestamp)
- RT-allocation guarantee unchanged: recycled_available() stays >0 for the timestamped path; cargo test passes

Depends on: Add an allocation-counting test harness asserting push_samples_or_drop is alloc-free in steady state

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Derive ring capacity from the negotiated device callback period
description:
calculate_capacity(requested,min) (ring_buffer.rs:653) returns a static 64-buffer default; backends call it with None (linux/mod.rs:406, macos/coreaudio.rs:186, macos/thread.rs:824) except WASAPI which passes config.buffer_size (wasapi.rs:544). Add calculate_capacity_for_period(period_frames, channels, sample_rate, headroom_buffers)->usize computing slot count covering N callback periods of headroom (8-16x), rounded to next power of two, clamped min/max. Wire backends to pass the negotiated period: WASAPI GetBufferSize, PipeWire negotiated buffer size (linux/thread.rs ~1145), CoreAudio IOProc frame count. Fall back to 64 when unknown. Reduces avoidable drops under scheduling jitter; validate via the capacity-sweep benchmark.

Acceptance criteria:
- calculate_capacity_for_period is a pure function with unit tests covering period->capacity mapping, power-of-two rounding, min/max clamping
- At least WASAPI is wired to the period-aware calculator; Linux/macOS wired where period is available else documented fallback to 64
- Existing calculate_capacity and test_calculate_capacity remain unchanged and passing
- Capacity-sweep benchmark shows fewer drops at the period-derived capacity vs the static 64 default under a simulated bursty consumer

Depends on: Add criterion benchmark harness + benches/ scaffolding for the bridge data plane

(effort: medium | verify: windows-native | wave: 3)

title: Cache-pad BridgeShared diagnostic atomics to remove false-sharing tail latency
description:
BridgeShared (ring_buffer.rs:115-152) packs buffers_pushed/dropped/popped/consecutive_drops/negotiated atomics adjacently; the producer writes pushed/dropped/consecutive every callback while the consumer writes popped every pop, so on 32+ core systems they share a cache line and ping-pong (false sharing), adding p99 jitter to the RT push. rtrb pads its own head/tail internally but rsac's extended counters are unpadded. Introduce a small internal CachePadded<T> newtype (or vendor rtrb's pattern / use crossbeam-utils if already in tree) and wrap producer-written vs consumer-written counters onto separate cache lines. Measure with an SPSC contention benchmark (producer + consumer threads, pin if possible) comparing p50/p99 push latency padded vs unpadded.

Acceptance criteria:
- Producer-mutated and consumer-mutated counters are on distinct cache lines via a CachePadded wrapper (alignment >=64 bytes asserted in a test)
- All existing BridgeShared counter reads and their tests are unchanged in behavior
- An SPSC contention benchmark reports p50/p99 push latency padded vs unpadded; padded is no worse and ideally improves p99
- No new heavy dependency added unless already present (prefer a tiny internal newtype)

Depends on: Add criterion benchmark harness + benches/ scaffolding for the bridge data plane

(effort: small | verify: bench | wave: 3)

title: Add a panic guard at the OS callback boundary in the producer push methods
description:
The OS audio callback invokes producer.push_samples_or_drop(...) directly (macos/thread.rs:284, windows/thread.rs:517, linux/thread.rs:1118). A panic on that path unwinds across the OS/C audio engine boundary -> UB / corrupted engine. Add a catch_unwind-wrapped push_samples_guarded (or wrap at the call sites) that on panic logs once, increments buffers_dropped, and best-effort transitions state to Error via the existing AtomicStreamState rather than unwinding (screencapturekit-rs panic_safe pattern). catch_unwind on the happy path is near-free; ensure no allocation is introduced (alloc-probe must still show 0). RT-safety hardening, no DSP.

Acceptance criteria:
- A guarded push path wraps the producer push in std::panic::catch_unwind and never lets a panic cross the FFI/OS callback boundary
- On a forced panic (instrumented test path) the stream transitions to Error and the drop counter increments instead of aborting/UB
- The guard adds no heap allocation on the non-panicking path (alloc-probe passes)
- At least one platform call site (or the producer method itself) uses the guarded path; happy-path behavior unchanged

Depends on: Add an allocation-counting test harness asserting push_samples_or_drop is alloc-free in steady state

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Expose PlatformCapabilities::SUPPORTED_SAMPLE_RATES as a public const
description:
Promote the private const SUPPORTED_SAMPLE_RATES:[u32;6] inside build() (api.rs:157) to a single public source of truth on PlatformCapabilities (src/core/capabilities.rs): pub const SUPPORTED_SAMPLE_RATES:[u32;6]=[22050,32000,44100,48000,88200,96000]; and pub fn supported_sample_rates()->&'static [u32]. Update build() to reference PlatformCapabilities::SUPPORTED_SAMPLE_RATES, eliminating the duplication so callers can pre-validate against the same list. Additive, const, zero runtime cost.

Acceptance criteria:
- PlatformCapabilities::SUPPORTED_SAMPLE_RATES is public and equals [22050,32000,44100,48000,88200,96000]
- build() references the public const (no second local literal array remains — grep finds a single definition)
- supported_sample_rates() returns the same slice; a test asserts 48000 present, 11025 absent
- Existing builder_accepts_all_supported_sample_rates (api.rs:1123) and builder_rejects_nonstandard_sample_rate still pass

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 4)

title: Add a capture! declarative macro for one-line builder construction
description:
Add #[macro_export] macro_rules! capture in src/lib.rs (re-exported from prelude via pub use crate::capture;) flattening the AudioCaptureBuilder chain, e.g. capture!(target: CaptureTarget::SystemDefault, sample_rate: 48000, channels: 2) expanding to AudioCaptureBuilder::new().with_target(...).sample_rate(...).channels(...) and returning the builder (caller chooses .build()/.start()). Support an optional target_str: 'app:1234' arm expanding to .target_str(...). All fields optional, order-independent via named keys. The macro must reference only public builder methods so it works for downstreams.

Acceptance criteria:
- capture!(target: CaptureTarget::SystemDefault) yields a builder whose target is SystemDefault
- capture!(sample_rate: 44100, channels: 1) sets those fields; omitting a field leaves the default
- capture!(target_str: 'app:99') produces a builder; .build()/.start() reachable
- Exported from crate root and usable via use rsac::prelude::*; (compile test in an integration test or doctest outside the crate-internal module proves only public API is used)

Depends on: Add AudioCaptureBuilder::target_str() string-target setter; Add rsac::prelude module with explicit re-exports

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 4)

title: Add optional tracing feature with lifecycle spans/events (L1)
description:
Replace ad-hoc log:: calls on the lifecycle with structured, correlatable instrumentation gated behind a new opt-in tracing Cargo feature (tracing = { version='0.1', optional=true }; tracing = ['dep:tracing']; default OFF). Introduce one internal macro module (src/utils/trace.rs) rsac_event!/rsac_span! expanding to tracing::event!/span! when the feature is on and falling back to log:: otherwise — exactly one instrumentation call site per event regardless of feature. Instrument only high-value non-RT lifecycle points: AudioCaptureBuilder::build() (target + negotiated format), AudioCapture::start()/stop()/Drop (api.rs:450,588,938), and the backpressure-trip transition. Attach a process-unique capture_id assigned at build() to each capture's span. EXPLICITLY do not instrument the OS callback / push path.

Acceptance criteria:
- The tracing feature compiles on and off; default build does NOT pull in tracing
- rsac_event!/rsac_span! has a single definition and falls back to log:: when disabled (inspection + a compile/doctest under each cfg)
- build()/start()/stop()/Drop emit an event carrying capture_id and (where relevant) the negotiated format; no rsac_event! appears in audio/*/thread.rs (inspection)
- capture_id is unique per AudioCapture within a process
- cargo build --features tracing and cargo build (default) both succeed; cargo test passes

Depends on: Add AudioCapture::format() exposing the negotiated delivery format

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: Criterion benchmark + observability snapshot for stats/backpressure read path
description:
Add a criterion bench under benches/ measuring the new read-path observability so it stays cheap and never touches the RT path: (a) AudioCapture::stream_stats() / backpressure_report() latency over a warmed bridge, (b) AudioBuffer::rms()/peak()/rms_dbfs() throughput at a 10ms@48kHz stereo buffer, (c) a regression guard that a tight push_samples_or_drop loop shows the same allocation behavior with the new windowed counters as without (compare recycled_available steady-state invariant, paralleling steady_state_push_samples_pop_loop at ring_buffer.rs:1061). Verification-only; no library API change. Document expected order-of-magnitude in the bench header.

Acceptance criteria:
- benches/observability.rs exists and is registered as a [[bench]]; cargo bench --no-run builds it
- Bench covers stream_stats(), backpressure_report(), and AudioBuffer metering on a realistic buffer
- A test re-asserts the RT producer stays alloc-free with windowed counters enabled (recycled_available()>0 after a steady push/pop loop)
- No public API changes introduced by this task

Depends on: Implement AudioCapture::stream_stats() and complete the StreamStats struct (H3); Add RT-safe windowed drop-rate tracking and BackpressureReport (M6); Add zero-alloc AudioBuffer metering: rms/peak/dBFS, channel-strided, NaN-safe (H2); Add criterion benchmark harness + benches/ scaffolding for the bridge data plane

(effort: small | verify: bench | wave: 4)

title: Bump wasapi 0.22->0.23 and adopt safe WAVEFORMATEXTENSIBLE blob parsing
description:
Verified wasapi='0.22.0' pinned. Bump wasapi='0.23.0' in Cargo.toml (and bindings/rsac-ffi if it pins it). 0.23 bumps MSRV to 1.76 (well under the pinned 1.95.0 toolchain) — add rust-version='1.76' to [package] to make the floor explicit. Audit src/audio/windows/wasapi.rs query_supported_formats_internal() (lines 596-705): prefer 0.23's WaveFormat::parse_from_blob_bytes() (uses read_unaligned) over any aligned cast, eliminating unaligned-read UB. Keep get_mixformat() + is_supported() probe loop and wasapi_bits_to_sample_format (wasapi.rs:907) intact. No public API change.

Acceptance criteria:
- Cargo.toml declares wasapi='0.23.0' and [package].rust-version='1.76'
- cargo check --no-default-features --features feat_windows compiles on the 1.95.0 toolchain with zero new warnings

- Existing wasapi.rs unit tests pass on Windows (test_audio_device_supported_formats, test_default_device_returns_device)

(effort: medium | verify: windows-native | wave: 2)

title: Add AudioDevice::kind() fallible default + uniform DeviceKind across all three backends
description:
Extend the AudioDevice trait (src/core/interface.rs:41) with a NEW provided method fn kind(&self)->AudioResult<DeviceKind> defaulting to Err(AudioError::PlatformNotSupported{feature:'device kind',..}) (additive/non-breaking for external impls). Implement per backend: Windows already has inherent fallible WindowsAudioDevice::kind() (wasapi.rs:747 via IMMEndpoint::GetDataFlow) — trait method delegates (resolves the M1 api_fit clash by staying fallible). Linux LinuxAudioDevice (audio/linux/mod.rs:359) carries is_input/is_output — map to Input/Output (Input wins if both, documented). macOS MacosAudioDevice (coreaudio.rs:131) holds only device_id — probe scope via kAudioDevicePropertyStreams / scopeInput vs Output; Err if neither scope has streams. DeviceKind already exists/exported (interface.rs:19, lib.rs:107).

Acceptance criteria:
- AudioDevice gains provided kind()->AudioResult<DeviceKind> with a PlatformNotSupported default (no external-impl breakage)
- Windows trait kind() delegates to IMMEndpoint::GetDataFlow (Output for render, Input for capture)
- Linux kind() maps is_output->Output / is_input->Input with a documented tie-break
- macOS kind() probes CoreAudio stream scope returning Input/Output, or Err when indeterminate
- Per-platform unit test asserts the default device's kind() is Ok and matches the loopback expectation

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Add DeviceInfo snapshot + AudioDevice::describe() + additive AudioSourceKind::Device kind field
description:
Add pub struct DeviceInfo { pub id: DeviceId, pub name: String, pub kind: DeviceKind, pub is_default: bool, pub default_format: Option<AudioFormat> } (#[non_exhaustive]) in src/core/interface.rs, plus a provided AudioDevice::describe(&self)->DeviceInfo whose default composes id()/name()/is_default()/kind()/supported_formats().first(). Export DeviceInfo from lib.rs. Extend AudioSourceKind::Device (introspection.rs:42) ADDITIVELY with kind: Option<DeviceKind> (Option because Linux supported_formats is empty by design and kind probing can fail). Populate in list_audio_sources (introspection.rs:144) via dev.kind().ok(). Update the to_capture_target() match (introspection.rs:69) to ignore the new field. Doc-note that default_format is None on Linux by design (L2).

Acceptance criteria:
- DeviceInfo defined #[non_exhaustive] and re-exported from lib.rs
- AudioDevice::describe() default returns a DeviceInfo from existing trait methods; no backend must override it to compile
- AudioSourceKind::Device gains kind: Option<DeviceKind> without breaking to_capture_target() (the arm ignores the new field)
- list_audio_sources populates kind via dev.kind().ok() and existing introspection tests still pass
- Doc note records that default_format is None on Linux by design

Depends on: Add AudioDevice::kind() fallible default + uniform DeviceKind across all three backends

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: macOS: enumerate all devices with multi-format probing and richer metadata
description:
MacosAudioDevice::supported_formats (coreaudio.rs:149) returns at most ONE format (single kAudioDevicePropertyStreamFormat query). Replace with per-stream probing: query kAudioDevicePropertyStreams for the output scope, then kAudioStreamPropertyAvailableVirtualFormats (or PhysicalFormats) per stream, converting each AudioStreamBasicDescription via the existing asbd_to_audio_format() (coreaudio.rs:342). De-duplicate. Gives macOS parity with the Windows multi-format list. MacosDeviceEnumerator::enumerate_devices (coreaudio.rs:240) already lists all device IDs — keep that; format probing is the real gap. Enumeration/init path only (RT-irrelevant).

Acceptance criteria:
- supported_formats returns >1 AudioFormat on a device advertising multiple virtual formats (probed via kAudioDevicePropertyStreams + AvailableVirtualFormats)
- Each probed ASBD is converted with the existing asbd_to_audio_format; non-LinearPCM/big-endian formats are skipped (not errored)
- Returns the current stream format first (stable ordering) and never panics on a zero-stream device (returns vec![])
- enumerate_devices continues to return all CoreAudio device IDs; a macOS test asserts the default output device reports >=1 format and supported_formats does not panic on any enumerated device

(effort: medium | verify: macos-ci | wave: 3)

title: macOS: filter enumerate_audio_applications to processes actually producing audio
description:
enumerate_audio_applications (coreaudio.rs:79) returns EVERY running NSWorkspace application, flooding list_audio_sources with non-audio processes. Add a CoreAudio-backed filter: enumerate audio process objects via kAudioHardwarePropertyProcessObjectList, keep only those with kAudioProcessPropertyIsRunning (or IsRunningInput/Output) true, intersecting against the NSWorkspace PID set to recover localizedName/bundleId. Keep an unfiltered enumerate_audio_applications_all() for diagnostics; make list_audio_applications_into (introspection.rs:175) consume the filtered set. macOS 14.4+ only (reuse get_macos_version gate); older macOS falls back to current behavior with a doc note.

Acceptance criteria:
- enumerate_audio_applications returns only PIDs with an active CoreAudio process object (IsRunning) on macOS 14.4+, each carrying name + bundle_id from NSWorkspace
- On macOS <14.4 it falls back to the existing NSWorkspace enumeration (documented)
- list_audio_sources no longer lists obviously non-audio background processes (assert count < raw NSWorkspace count, or a known non-audio helper is absent)
- No panic when the process object list property is unavailable (returns the NSWorkspace fallback)

(effort: medium | verify: macos-ci | wave: 3)

title: Add supports_device_change_notifications capability flag + honest macOS sub-14.4 fields
description:
Add three additive fields to PlatformCapabilities (src/core/capabilities.rs:31): supports_device_change_notifications: bool, min_os_for_application_capture: Option<&'static str>, system_capture_available_via: &'static str. Populate in windows()/linux()/macos()/unsupported(). Set supports_device_change_notifications: true on Windows/macOS (native IMMNotificationClient / AudioObjectPropertyListener exist); Linux false until the M10 watch arm lands. Add PlatformCapabilities::explain_unsupported(&self, target:&CaptureTarget)->Option<String> returning a hint (e.g. macOS-13 Application -> 'requires macOS 14.4+; ScreenCaptureKit assessed, backend not yet implemented'). Phrase SCK strictly as an assessment, never a usable route. Update the supports_format_missing test literal (~capabilities.rs:383) and every struct-literal construction to include the new fields.

Acceptance criteria:
- PlatformCapabilities gains the 3 fields; all four constructors and every struct-literal test compile
- supports_device_change_notifications is true on Windows + macOS; Linux false until M10 watch lands
- explain_unsupported returns Some(hint) for Application/ProcessTree on macOS <14.4 and None when supported; SCK phrased as assessed-not-implemented
- min_os_for_application_capture is Some('14.4') on macOS, None on Windows/Linux
- cargo test passes the capability unit tests on all platforms (including the updated literal)

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Linux: native in-process PipeWire registry enumeration (H4 part 1 — devices + default)
description:
Build the native registry path replacing pw-cli/pw-dump/pw-metadata for DEVICE and DEFAULT discovery. In src/audio/linux/thread.rs the loop creates MainLoop+Context+Core+Registry but binds let _registry (thread.rs:887) and never listens. Add registry + metadata listeners populating an Rc<RefCell<snapshot>> on the PipeWire thread (global callbacks are Fn+'static — use the wiremix Rc<RefCell> idiom). Add additive PipeWireCommand::SnapshotDevices{reply} and SnapshotDefault{reply}, and PipeWireThread::snapshot_devices()/snapshot_default() that MUST wait for a core.sync()/done roundtrip before replying (else it races an empty registry). Define PwDeviceSnapshot { id (object.serial), name, media_class }. Route LinuxDeviceEnumerator::enumerate_devices and default_device (audio/linux/mod.rs:458/493) through a short-lived PipeWireThread spawn instead of std::process::Command. Keep the subprocess path behind a cfg/fallback until proven on headless/Flatpak. Only owned Vecs cross the mpsc reply; callbacks fire on the PW loop thread, never the audio callback.

Acceptance criteria:
- New PipeWireCommand::SnapshotDevices/SnapshotDefault + PipeWireThread accessors exist; reply waits for a core sync/done roundtrip
- enumerate_devices returns audio Node ids/serials from the native registry with NO std::process::Command on the native path
- Returned device ids are numeric object.serial values that round-trip through CaptureTarget::Device (PwNodeLookup::Device contract, audio/linux/mod.rs:303)
- With pw-dump REMOVED from PATH but PipeWire running, enumerate_devices still returns devices
- Empty native result still surfaces BackendError{backend:'PipeWire'} (L4 honest-failure), never a fabricated default; cargo test --features feat_linux passes; a new test asserts non-empty snapshot when PipeWire is available

(effort: large | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 2)

title: Linux: native in-process PipeWire application enumeration replacing pw-dump (H4 part 2)
description:
Replace the pw-dump subprocess in core/introspection.rs list_audio_applications_into (lines 209-268) with the native registry path from H4 part 1. Add PipeWireCommand::SnapshotApplications{reply} + PipeWireThread::snapshot_applications() returning PwAppSnapshot { pid, app_name, node_serial } built from registry Node globals whose props carry media.class containing 'Stream' and a parseable application.process.id (same predicate the subprocess parser uses at introspection.rs:215-247, but read from live registry props). Wire the Linux arm to call snapshot_applications via a short-lived discovery thread, dedup by PID exactly as today. Because introspection lives in core/ and must not depend on audio/ directly, route through the existing crate::audio facade (introspection.rs:137 calls crate::audio::get_device_enumerator — a sibling crate::audio::linux helper fn is the established precedent). Keep subprocess fallback behind cfg until proven.

Acceptance criteria:
- list_audio_applications() on Linux returns app sources from the native registry with NO pw-dump subprocess on the native path
- Same media.class contains 'Stream' + application.process.id predicate and PID-dedup as the subprocess parser
- With pw-dump removed from PATH but PipeWire running, list_audio_applications still returns running audio apps (not silently empty)
- core/ gains no direct dependency on audio/ internals — access via the crate::audio facade (DAG preserved)
- Existing introspection tests pass; a new test exercises the native app snapshot path when PipeWire is available

Depends on: Linux: native in-process PipeWire registry enumeration (H4 part 1 — devices + default)

(effort: large | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 3)

title: Linux: wire supported_formats() via PipeWire node enum_params (PR-5)
description:
With H4 landing the registry/proxy machinery, implement Linux supported_formats() as real format DISCOVERY (not conversion — in capture scope). For a LinuxAudioDevice id (object.serial), bind the Node proxy on the PipeWire thread and call enum_params for SPA_PARAM_EnumFormat, translating each spa audio format/rate/channel set to rsac AudioFormat. Add PipeWireCommand::EnumNodeFormats{serial,reply} + accessor; reply after a sync/done roundtrip. CRITICAL (EF-3 rejection / L2): this must NOT override the authoritative connect-time negotiation reported via param_changed — supported_formats is advisory discovery only; the delivered buffer's format remains whatever PipeWire negotiates. If enum_params yields nothing, return vec![] (preserving documented-empty behavior at audio/linux/mod.rs:373) rather than guessing. Update the L2 doc comment (audio/linux/mod.rs:373-382).

Acceptance criteria:
- supported_formats returns formats discovered via SPA_PARAM_EnumFormat for nodes that advertise them, else vec![] (never a fabricated guess)
- New PipeWireCommand::EnumNodeFormats + accessor wait for a sync/done roundtrip
- Connect-time param_changed negotiation remains authoritative — a test or doc assertion confirms supported_formats is advisory and does not alter delivered AudioBuffer.format()
- L2 doc comment updated to describe advisory discovery vs authoritative negotiation
- cargo test --features feat_linux: the supported_formats-empty fallback test still passes when enum_params yields nothing

Depends on: Linux: native in-process PipeWire registry enumeration (H4 part 1 — devices + default)

(effort: medium | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 4)

title: Add DeviceEvent + DeviceWatcher types and DeviceEnumerator::watch() default (M10 surface)
description:
Define the public hotplug/default-change surface in src/core/interface.rs: pub enum DeviceEvent { DeviceAdded{id,name,kind}, DeviceRemoved{id}, DefaultChanged{id,kind}, StateChanged{id,available} } (#[non_exhaustive]), and pub struct DeviceWatcher (RAII; Drop unregisters the OS listener + joins the notify thread). Add a PROVIDED trait method DeviceEnumerator::watch(&self, on_event: Box<dyn FnMut(DeviceEvent)+Send+'static>)->AudioResult<DeviceWatcher> defaulting to Err(PlatformNotSupported) (additive for external impls). Add an inherent CrossPlatformDeviceEnumerator::watch(...) (audio/mod.rs:62) that dispatches to per-backend impls (this inherent method is the real public entry — CrossPlatformDeviceEnumerator does not impl the trait). Export DeviceEvent + DeviceWatcher from lib.rs. Types + plumbing only; per-OS bodies are follow-on tasks. Unimplemented arms return PlatformNotSupported, consistent with supports_device_change_notifications=false.

Acceptance criteria:
- DeviceEvent (#[non_exhaustive]) + DeviceWatcher defined and exported from lib.rs
- DeviceEnumerator gains a provided watch() returning Err(PlatformNotSupported) by default (no external-impl breakage)
- CrossPlatformDeviceEnumerator::watch inherent method exists and dispatches per backend; unimplemented backends return PlatformNotSupported
- DeviceWatcher's Drop is documented to unregister the OS listener and join the notify thread; handler documented to run on the OS/PipeWire notify thread, never the audio callback
- cargo check on all three platforms compiles

Depends on: Add supports_device_change_notifications capability flag + honest macOS sub-14.4 fields

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: Windows: implement DeviceEnumerator::watch() via IMMNotificationClient (M10 Windows arm)
description:
Implement WindowsDeviceEnumerator::watch using RegisterEndpointNotificationCallback + an IMMNotificationClient COM callback (wasapi.rs, alongside WindowsDeviceEnumerator at line 790). Map OnDeviceAdded/OnDeviceRemoved/OnDefaultDeviceChanged/OnDeviceStateChanged to DeviceEvent. Deadlock-safety (cpal/CamillaDSP pattern): the COM callback must NOT invoke the user FnMut directly on the notification thread in a way that can deadlock COM — push a DeviceEvent into a bounded channel and run the user FnMut on a dedicated notify thread owned by DeviceWatcher; Drop calls UnregisterEndpointNotificationCallback and joins. WASAPI notification thread, not the audio callback — no allocation/lock policy violation.

Acceptance criteria:
- WindowsDeviceEnumerator::watch registers an IMMNotificationClient and returns a DeviceWatcher
- OnDefaultDeviceChanged/OnDeviceAdded/OnDeviceRemoved/OnDeviceStateChanged translate to the matching DeviceEvent variants delivered to the user FnMut on a non-COM-notification helper thread (bounded-channel hand-off)
- DeviceWatcher::drop calls UnregisterEndpointNotificationCallback and joins the notify thread with no leak/hang
- Windows integration test: register a watcher, toggle the default device (or assert no crash over a short live window), confirm clean drop
- supports_device_change_notifications remains true on Windows

Depends on: Add DeviceEvent + DeviceWatcher types and DeviceEnumerator::watch() default (M10 surface)

(effort: large | verify: windows-native | wave: 5)

title: macOS: implement DeviceEnumerator::watch() via AudioObjectPropertyListener (M10 macOS arm)
description:
Implement MacosDeviceEnumerator::watch (coreaudio.rs:239) by registering AudioObjectAddPropertyListener on kAudioObjectSystemObject for kAudioHardwarePropertyDevices (add/remove) and kAudioHardwarePropertyDefaultOutputDevice / DefaultInputDevice (DefaultChanged). On each callback, diff the current device-id set against the previous snapshot to emit DeviceAdded/DeviceRemoved, and emit DefaultChanged with the new default's id + kind. The listener runs on a CoreAudio-managed thread; forward DeviceEvent to the user FnMut via a bounded-channel + helper thread hand-off (matching the Windows arm). DeviceWatcher::drop must AudioObjectRemovePropertyListener for every registered address (RAII). Reuse asbd/kind probing from the kind() task to populate DeviceAdded.kind.

Acceptance criteria:
- MacosDeviceEnumerator::watch registers listeners for device-list and default-output/input changes and returns a DeviceWatcher
- Adding/removing an aggregate or virtual device (e.g. BlackHole) emits DeviceAdded/DeviceRemoved; changing the default emits DefaultChanged with id + kind
- DeviceWatcher::drop removes every registered property listener (no dangling listener, no leak)
- macOS integration test: register a watcher and assert clean registration + drop over a short window without panic
- DeviceAdded.kind is populated via the shared kind-probing helper

Depends on: Add DeviceEvent + DeviceWatcher types and DeviceEnumerator::watch() default (M10 surface); Add AudioDevice::kind() fallible default + uniform DeviceKind across all three backends

(effort: large | verify: macos-ci | wave: 5)

title: Linux: implement DeviceEnumerator::watch() via persistent PipeWire registry/metadata listener (M10 Linux arm)
description:
Implement the Linux watch arm on top of the H4 native registry infrastructure. LinuxDeviceEnumerator is a unit struct with no persistent loop, so watch() must spawn a NEW persistent PipeWire main-loop + registry + metadata listener thread (distinct from the per-call snapshot threads in H4). Registry global/global_remove callbacks -> DeviceAdded/DeviceRemoved (filtered to audio Node media.class); the default.audio.sink/source Metadata listener -> DefaultChanged. Forward DeviceEvent to the user FnMut. DeviceWatcher::drop signals the loop to quit and joins (RAII). Callbacks fire on the PW loop thread (Fn+'static, Rc<RefCell> idiom), never the audio callback. Flip supports_device_change_notifications to true for Linux in capabilities.rs::linux() ONLY in this task.

Acceptance criteria:
- LinuxDeviceEnumerator::watch spawns a persistent pw main-loop+registry+metadata listener thread and returns a DeviceWatcher
- Registry add/remove of audio Nodes emit DeviceAdded/DeviceRemoved; default.audio.sink/source metadata changes emit DefaultChanged
- DeviceWatcher::drop quits the loop and joins the thread (no leaked thread, no hang)
- capabilities.rs::linux() now sets supports_device_change_notifications: true
- WSL/Linux test with PipeWire available: register a watcher, load/unload a null sink (pactl), observe DeviceAdded/DeviceRemoved; clean drop

Depends on: Add DeviceEvent + DeviceWatcher types and DeviceEnumerator::watch() default (M10 surface); Linux: native in-process PipeWire registry enumeration (H4 part 1 — devices + default)

(effort: large | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 5)

title: Add a cross-platform enumeration non-empty / honest-failure test matrix + DeviceInfo round-trip
description:
Lock in the enumeration contract (audit L4). Add a tests/ci_audio module (or extend tests/ci_audio/main.rs) that, per platform with audio available: (a) asserts get_device_enumerator().enumerate_devices() is Ok(non-empty) OR the documented honest BackendError — never Ok(empty), never a fabricated 'default' id; (b) asserts every returned device's describe() yields a DeviceInfo whose to_capture_target() round-trips (DeviceId resolvable back through CaptureTarget::Device); (c) asserts kind() is Ok on the default device; (d) on Linux, asserts the native path works with pw-dump removed from PATH. Provide a shared assert_enumeration_honest!() macro so each backend's test is one line. Inspection/CI gates, not product code.

Acceptance criteria:
- A shared assert_enumeration_honest! macro asserts non-empty-or-honest-BackendError and no fabricated default id, used by all three backend tests
- DeviceInfo round-trip test: describe().to_capture_target() yields a CaptureTarget::Device whose id matches the source device id for every enumerated device
- default device kind() returns Ok in the per-platform test
- Linux test variant runs with pw-dump removed from PATH and still passes via the native registry path
- Tests are wired into the ci_audio harness and skip cleanly (eprintln) when no audio stack is present (matching pipewire_available() gating)

Depends on: Add DeviceInfo snapshot + AudioDevice::describe() + additive AudioSourceKind::Device kind field; Linux: native in-process PipeWire registry enumeration (H4 part 1 — devices + default)

(effort: medium | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 4)

title: Bump transitive enumeration deps and remove serde_json from the native Linux path
description:
Two parts. (1) Dep hygiene: confirm sysinfo (0.38.4) and serde_json (1.0.149, used ONLY to parse pw-dump JSON in introspection.rs:213 and bin/) are current; bump to latest compatible patch and re-run the suite. (2) Once H4 parts 1+2 land, serde_json is no longer needed on the native Linux enumeration path — remove the pw-dump JSON parse once the subprocess fallback is retired, or scope serde_json to dev/diagnostics-only if still used by bin/pipewire_diagnostics. Gated behind H4 so the fallback is deleted only after native is proven.

Acceptance criteria:

- After H4 lands, the pw-dump JSON parse in introspection.rs is removed (or the subprocess fallback is cfg-gated) and serde_json is no longer pulled into the default Linux library build (verify cargo tree --features feat_linux)

title: Define target-conditional feature presets shared across rsac-ffi/rsac-napi/rsac-python
description:
Establish one feature-resolution pattern for all binding Cargo.toml files: each depends on rsac with default-features=false and selects the host backend via [target.'cfg(...)'.dependencies.rsac] blocks (feat_windows on windows, feat_linux on linux, feat_macos on macos), plus passthrough sink-wav/async-stream where exposed. rsac-ffi already mirrors features manually (rsac-ffi/Cargo.toml:13-19) but rsac-napi (rsac-napi/Cargo.toml:13 pulls implicit default = all three backends, bloating non-host builds) and rsac-python (hardcoded feat_macos) diverge. Document the convention in bindings/README or AGENTS so future bindings copy it. Grounding for the publishability + cross-compile tasks.

Acceptance criteria:
- rsac-ffi, rsac-napi, rsac-python each compile only the host platform's backend by default (a Linux build does not pull in windows/coreaudio crates)
- An explicit opt-in (e.g. --features all-backends) still exists where docs.rs-style all-platform compile is needed
- Convention documented in one place referenced by all three manifests

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Make rsac-ffi publishable to crates.io
description:
In bindings/rsac-ffi/Cargo.toml remove publish=false (line 7) and add crates.io-required metadata (repository, readme, keywords, categories) mirroring the root Cargo.toml. The rsac path dependency (line 22) must gain a version requirement (rsac = { path='../../', version='0.2', default-features=false }) so cargo publish resolves against the published crate. Adopt the target-conditional default features (feature-preset task) so a bare build/publish compiles the host backend. Add rsac-ffi to the release.yml publish job AFTER the rsac crate publishes (workflow currently publishes only the root crate per release.yml:14-23).

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo publish --dry-run -p rsac-ffi succeeds (no publish=false, all required metadata present)
- rsac dependency carries both path and version so it resolves post-publish
- release.yml publishes rsac-ffi as a step gated on the rsac crate publish succeeding first
- include/rsac.h is included in the published package (verify package contents in dry-run output)

Depends on: Define target-conditional feature presets shared across rsac-ffi/rsac-napi/rsac-python

(effort: medium | verify: inspection | wave: 3)

title: Eliminate f64 expansion in NAPI AudioChunk: expose interleaved f32 via Float32Array
description:
In bindings/rsac-napi/src/lib.rs the AudioChunk.data field is Vec<f64> (line 39) populated by buf.data().iter().map(|&s| s as f64).collect() (line 56) — a per-buffer allocation + f32->f64 doubling on the delivery path (start_data_pump at lib.rs:478 and the read* methods). Change the JS-facing representation to carry f32 directly: replace the #[napi(object)] struct field with a napi Float32Array/TypedArray<f32> (or Buffer over the f32 byte view) built from buf.data() so JS receives a native Float32Array with no precision-widening copy. Update index.d.ts. Keep num_frames/channels/sample_rate/length/duration. Note the type change in CHANGELOG and bump NAPI minor.

Acceptance criteria:
- AudioChunk no longer allocates a Vec<f64>; JS receives a Float32Array (or f32-backed Buffer) over the captured samples
- node --test passes: a captured chunk's data.length == num_frames*channels and values match the source f32 within exact equality
- index.d.ts updated to reflect Float32Array; README example still compiles
- No f32->f64 conversion remains in from_rsac_buffer

(effort: medium | verify: inspection | wave: 2)

title: Fix #30: replace unaligned f32->u8 reinterpret in Python to_bytes() with a sound conversion
description:
In bindings/rsac-python/src/lib.rs the to_bytes() method (lines 316-322) does std::slice::from_raw_parts(data.as_ptr() as *const u8, data.len()*4). While &[f32]->&[u8] is alignment-safe (u8 align=1), the pattern is the flagged #30 align_to concern and is fragile if copy-pasted into the reverse direction. Replace with a provably-sound conversion: depend on bytemuck and use bytemuck::cast_slice::<f32,u8>(data) (preferred — zero-copy, statically sound, removes the unsafe block), or build via data.iter().flat_map(|s| s.to_le_bytes()). Document the little-endian f32 contract in the docstring.

Acceptance criteria:
- to_bytes() contains no hand-rolled from_raw_parts reinterpret; uses bytemuck::cast_slice or an explicitly-safe path
- Returned bytes length == len()*4 and round-trips: numpy.frombuffer(b, dtype='<f4') equals to_list()
- cargo build -p rsac-python and the Python smoke test pass; no clippy warning on the conversion
- GitHub issue #30 referenced in the commit/PR and closed

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Fix #28: harden Go callback path against use-after-free of the C buffer pointer
description:
Audit and lock down the #28 Go UAF in bindings/rsac-go/callback.go and rsac.go. goAudioCallback (callback.go:24-65) already copies C f32 data into Go memory before invoking the user fn — good — but verify two hazards: (1) the cgo.Handle lifecycle in SetCallback/clearCallbackHandleLocked (callback.go:91-110) must guarantee the handle is never resolved after Delete() during a concurrent in-flight callback (a callback firing on the FFI delivery thread while Close() runs). Introduce ordering so rsac_capture_set_callback(NULL)/stop is issued and drained BEFORE the cgo.Handle is Deleted, or guard resolution. (2) audioBufferFromC (rsac.go:281-313) copies then frees the C buffer via defer — confirm no Go AudioBuffer aliases the C memory after free. Add a stress test registering a callback and start/stop/closing rapidly under -race.

Acceptance criteria:
- Callback delete ordering ensures no cgo.Handle.Value() resolves after Delete() (clear-callback at the C layer precedes handle Delete)
- go test -race of a start/callback/stop/close churn test passes with no race or UAF report
- audioBufferFromC verified to leave no Go slice aliasing freed C memory (data is always make()+copy)
- GitHub issue #28 referenced and closed

(effort: medium | verify: inspection | wave: 2)

title: Add FFI stats accessors: rsac_capture_stream_stats() + rsac_capture_format()
description:
Once AudioCapture::stream_stats()/format() exist, expose them through the C ABI in bindings/rsac-ffi/src/lib.rs. Add a #[repr(C)] struct rsac_stream_stats_t { overruns:u64, buffers_captured:u64, buffers_dropped:u64, uptime_secs:f64, is_running:u8 } and rsac_capture_stream_stats(capture:*const RsacCapture, out:*mut rsac_stream_stats_t)->rsac_error_t (null-checked, catch()-wrapped like the existing functions at lib.rs:484+). Add rsac_capture_format(capture, *mut rsac_audio_format_t) mirroring AudioFormat (sample_rate u32, channels u16, sample_format enum). Regenerate include/rsac_generated.h (build.rs:7-16 runs cbindgen) and hand-mirror into include/rsac.h. ABI additive — only new symbols.

Acceptance criteria:
- New C functions compile, are catch()-guarded and null-checked, and appear in the regenerated rsac_generated.h
- rsac_capture_stream_stats returns overruns matching rsac_capture_overrun_count(); buffers_dropped/uptime are populated
- include/rsac.h hand-mirror updated in lockstep (the Go binding's ABI source)
- Existing FFI symbols unchanged (no ABI break)

Depends on: Implement AudioCapture::stream_stats() and complete the StreamStats struct (H3)

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: Fix #29: SHA-pin all third-party GitHub Actions in ci.yml
description:
ci.yml pins actions by floating tag (actions/checkout@v5, Swatinem/rust-cache@v2, taiki-e/install-action@v2 — ci.yml:46,50,370 etc.), a supply-chain risk. release.yml already pins by full 40-char SHA with a version comment (release.yml:82-86). Apply the same convention to ci.yml: replace every uses: <action>@v* with uses: <action>@<full-sha> # vX for all third-party actions (first-party actions/* may follow the same policy for consistency). Reuse the exact SHAs already in release.yml where the same action+version is used.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every third-party uses: in ci.yml is pinned to a full 40-hex SHA with a trailing # vX comment
- SHAs for shared actions match those already pinned in release.yml
- ci.yml still parses and the workflow runs green on a no-op push
- GitHub issue #29 referenced and closed

(effort: small | verify: inspection | wave: 2)

title: Surface stream_stats()/format() in Python, Node, and Go bindings
description:
Propagate the stats/format facade to the higher-level bindings so downstreams stop polling only overrun_count. Python (rsac-python/src/lib.rs): add stream_stats() returning a frozen PyStreamStats pyclass (overruns, buffers_captured, buffers_dropped, uptime_secs, is_running) and a format getter returning a namedtuple (sample_rate, channels, sample_format) on PyAudioCapture (near overrun_count at lib.rs:660). Node (rsac-napi/src/lib.rs): add streamStats(): StreamStats (#[napi(object)] JsStreamStats) and a format getter, next to overrun_count at lib.rs:461. Go (rsac.go): add (c *AudioCapture) StreamStats()(StreamStats,error) and Format()(Format,error) calling the new FFI functions, mirroring OverrunCount at rsac.go:541. Update each binding's .pyi/.d.ts/doc.

Acceptance criteria:
- Python: cap.stream_stats() returns overruns/buffers_captured/buffers_dropped/uptime_secs/is_running; cap.format returns sample_rate/channels/sample_format; .pyi updated
- Node: capture.streamStats() and capture.format exist and are typed in index.d.ts; node --test asserts fields present
- Go: StreamStats()/Format() return populated structs; go test asserts OverrunCount() == StreamStats().Overruns
- All three build against the new FFI/Rust symbols with no ABI mismatch

Depends on: Add FFI stats accessors: rsac_capture_stream_stats() + rsac_capture_format()

(effort: medium | verify: inspection | wave: 4)

description:
With core FromStr landed (H1), give each binding a single string entry point so downstreams stop hand-rolling target parsing. Go: add ParseTarget(s string)(CaptureTarget,error) and CaptureBuilder.WithTargetString(s) (rsac.go:343) deferring to rsac_builder_set_target_str. Keep all existing typed constructors (additive).

Acceptance criteria:

description:
With core AudioBuffer metering landed (H2), delete the duplicated hand-rolled rms/peak in rsac-python/src/lib.rs:335-353 (which re-iterates samples) and call the new core methods; add rms()/peak()/rms_dbfs()/peak_dbfs() to NAPI AudioChunk and Go AudioBuffer (rsac.go:232 methods block) so all bindings get metering for free. title: Establish a versioning + ABI-change contract across rsac and the four bindings
description:
Bindings version independently with no breakage tracking (rsac 0.2.0, rsac-ffi 0.1.0, rsac-python 0.2.0, rsac-napi 0.2.0, rsac-go has no version field — go.mod:1-3). Define and document the policy in docs/RELEASE_PROCESS.md (referenced by release.yml:1-23): (a) all crates bump in lockstep on a semver tag; (b) any change to the C ABI (rsac-ffi exported symbols / rsac.h) is a MAJOR for rsac-ffi and requires a CHANGELOG ABI section; (c) tag rsac-go releases (git tags bindings/rsac-go/vX.Y.Z) and record the module path. Add an ABI-changes subsection to CHANGELOG.md and a CI check (extend scripts/bump-version.sh) asserting all four binding manifests carry the same version on a release tag.

Acceptance criteria:
- RELEASE_PROCESS.md documents lockstep bump, ABI-major rule for rsac-ffi, and rsac-go tag convention
- CHANGELOG.md has an explicit 'C ABI changes' subsection format
- A CI/script check fails if rsac-ffi/rsac-python/rsac-napi versions diverge from the release tag
- rsac-go release tagging documented (module path + tag shape)

title: Build PyPI wheels for rsac-python across the platform matrix (cibuildwheel/maturin, abi3-py39)
description:

Add maturin abi3 (features=['pyo3/abi3-py39'] in pyproject [tool.maturin]) so one wheel per platform covers 3.9+ (rsac#18). Each platform wheel compiles only its backend (depends on the feature-preset task).

Acceptance criteria:

title: Linux Blacksmith Deterministic PipeWire E2E
description:
# CI E2E Audio Seeds - 2026-07-02

Purpose: define end-to-end coverage for rsac and capture runner-dependent work that should not be merged as speculative CI gates without platform evidence.

## E2E Definition

An rsac end-to-end gate means: build the library, CLI/examples, and publishable bindings; provision a deterministic virtual audio route; play a generated 440 Hz tone; capture through the public API/CLI or binding surface; assert non-silence, RMS, 440 Hz tone dominance, negotiated format, and terminal lifecycle behavior after stop/request_stop/drop.

Target capture modes:

- `SystemDefault`
- `Device(DeviceId)` against the virtual monitor/input endpoint
- `Application(ApplicationId)` using the spawned tone player PID
- `ApplicationByName` using the exact runner-observed process/application name
- `ProcessTree(ProcessId)` rooted at the tone player parent

## Seed: Linux Blacksmith Deterministic PipeWire E2E

Current status: `.github/workflows/ci-audio-tests.yml` creates a PipeWire/Pulse null sink and monitor on `blacksmith-4vcpu-ubuntu-2404`, but the workflow intentionally does not set `RSAC_CI_AUDIO_DETERMINISTIC=1` because dbus-less Firecracker PipeWire routing has previously exposed `ci_test_sink.monitor` while `SystemDefault` still yielded zero buffers.

Do not harden Linux content assertions until this evidence exists:

- `pw-cli info 0` ready after manual `pipewire`, `wireplumber`, and `pipewire-pulse` startup.
- `pactl list short sinks` shows only or default-routed `ci_test_sink`.
- `pactl list short sources` shows `ci_test_sink.monitor`.
- A spawned player (`pw-play`, `paplay`, or `sox play`) is explicitly routed to `ci_test_sink` using `PULSE_SINK=ci_test_sink` or a verified PipeWire node link.
- `cargo test --test ci_audio system_capture --no-default-features --features feat_linux -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture` captures non-silent buffers and detects the 440 Hz tone with `RSAC_CI_AUDIO_DETERMINISTIC=1`.

Workflow sketch once proven:

```yaml
- name: Route test tone to PipeWire null sink
  run: |
    PULSE_SINK=ci_test_sink pw-play /tmp/test_440hz.wav &
    pw-dump --no-colors > pipewire-before-test.json
    echo "RSAC_CI_AUDIO_DETERMINISTIC=1" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
```

Acceptance criteria:

- Linux system, device, application PID, application name, process tree, subscribe, overrun, and lifecycle tests hard-fail on missing tone only after the deterministic route is proven green.
- Preserve soft assertions when `RSAC_CI_AUDIO_DETERMINISTIC` is unset.

---

closeReason: Closed by PR #43 with rsac-b106 (same change set): RSAC_CI_AUDIO_DETERMINISTIC=1 is now exported by the routing gate on all three Linux tiers after OS-level route proof, and the gate is HARD (a broken route fails at setup with pactl/wpctl/pw-dump diagnostics + artifact, never a soft false-green). Stability evidence per acceptance: consecutive green runs 28909318383 + 28909938840; helpers prefer paplay when PULSE_SINK is pinned so the proven route is the tested route.
