title: Node Binding Feature Selection Still Uses Root Defaults
description:
## P1 - Node Binding Feature Selection Still Uses Root Defaults

Finding: `rsac-napi` still depends on the root crate with implicit default features, while `docs/features.md` defines the canonical binding convention as per-target `rsac` dependencies with `default-features = false` and exactly one platform backend enabled.

Evidence:

- `bindings/rsac-napi/Cargo.toml:13` has `rsac = { path = "../.." }`.
- `docs/features.md:91` through `docs/features.md:153` defines the canonical binding feature-resolution convention.
- `docs/features.md:161` correctly still marks `rsac-napi` as `migrating` and says it should adopt per-target blocks.
- `bindings/rsac-python/Cargo.toml:30` through `bindings/rsac-python/Cargo.toml:43` is the working reference pattern.

Impact: the Node binding does not follow the project's own packaging contract. Even where target-specific dependency cfgs prevent wrong-OS native crates from linking, the binding enables the root all-backends meta-feature and can drift from the tested per-binding feature policy.

Recommended follow-up:

- Convert `bindings/rsac-napi/Cargo.toml` to the same per-target dependency pattern used by Python.
- If needed, add binding-local passthrough features only for features Node actually exposes.
- Update `docs/features.md:155` through `docs/features.md:165` once the manifest conforms.

Ready prompt:

```text
Current depth: 2. Maximum delegation depth: 5. Further nesting must not exceed depth 5. Do not push. Preserve unrelated dirty changes.

Migrate `bindings/rsac-napi/Cargo.toml` to the canonical per-target backend dependency convention documented in `docs/features.md`, using `bindings/rsac-python/Cargo.toml` as the reference. Keep the change manifest-only unless docs need the status line updated. Validate at minimum with `cargo check -p rsac-napi`; if on Windows, also run `cargo check -p rsac-napi --no-default-features` only if the package defines such features after the change. Do not broaden feature surfaces unnecessarily.
```

## P1 - Release And Feature Docs Have Stale Version-Specific Claims

Finding: release/feature docs still carry stale 0.2-era statements even though manifests are at 0.4.0 and prior release-pipeline fixes have landed.

Evidence:

- `docs/features.md:190` says the matrix reflects the `0.2.0` release line.
- `docs/RELEASE_PROCESS.md:425` through `docs/RELEASE_PROCESS.md:429` says `rsac-ffi` trails at `0.1.0` while others are `0.2.0`; current manifests are `0.4.0`.
- `docs/RELEASE_PROCESS.md:431` through `docs/RELEASE_PROCESS.md:437` refers to the generated `rsac.h` header, while the actual generated twin is `bindings/rsac-ffi/include/rsac_generated.h` and curated consumer header is `bindings/rsac-ffi/include/rsac.h`.
- `scripts/bump-version.sh:3` through `scripts/bump-version.sh:9` and `.github/workflows/ci.yml:720` onward now reflect six-manifest lockstep, so the old warning is misleading.

Impact: release operators can distrust otherwise-correct automation, and C ABI guidance blurs the curated/generated header split that the CI header-drift check now relies on.

Recommended follow-up:

- Replace stale point-in-time statements with evergreen wording, or update them to 0.4.0 if they are intentionally historical.
- Clarify that `include/rsac.h` is curated and `include/rsac_generated.h` is the cbindgen output used for drift comparison.
- Keep 0.2.0 examples only where the section explicitly says they are examples.

Ready prompt:

```text
Current depth: 2. Maximum delegation depth: 5. Further nesting must not exceed depth 5. Do not push. Preserve unrelated dirty changes.

Refresh stale release/feature docs without changing code. Audit `docs/features.md`, `docs/RELEASE_PROCESS.md`, and nearby release docs for point-in-time 0.2.0 / rsac-ffi-0.1.0 claims that conflict with current 0.4.0 manifests and the curated-vs-generated C header policy. Make minimal wording updates only; keep worked examples as examples if clearly labeled. Validate with a grep for stale claims and, if available, markdown/link checks.
```

---
Migrated from manual seed markdown on 2026-07-02. Keep follow-up work in sd; do not recreate ad-hoc seed markdown.
closeReason: PR #46 squash-merged into release/0.4.1 as e3bb19b. Fixed: RELEASE_PROCESS ABI-(b) curated-vs-generated header split (rsac.h curated, rsac_generated.h cbindgen output for the CI drift check); features.md matrix pinned to evergreen 'the 0.4 line'. Seed's other 3 findings (features.md 0.2.0 line, rsac-ffi 0.1.0 trailing, pre-lockstep bump warning) verified already fixed by earlier release-stack layers; remaining 0.2.0 mentions are the explicitly-labeled worked example, kept per seed guidance.

title: Existing Seeds Already Cover Submodule, CI Matrix, And Platform Readiness Work
description:
## P2 - Existing Seeds Already Cover Submodule, CI Matrix, And Platform Readiness Work

This audit did not duplicate already-filed seeds for these areas:

- `apps/audio-graph` is configured in `.gitmodules` but absent as a gitlink/path: see `.seeds/delegation-backlog-2026-07-02.md:104` through `.seeds/delegation-backlog-2026-07-02.md:121`.
- CI coverage, deterministic Linux PipeWire routing, Windows runner boundaries, and macOS TCC/self-hosted planning are already seeded in `.seeds/delegation-backlog-2026-07-02.md:29` through `.seeds/delegation-backlog-2026-07-02.md:102`.
- Deferred dirty worktree imports are already seeded in `.seeds/delegation-backlog-2026-07-02.md:123` through `.seeds/delegation-backlog-2026-07-02.md:140`.

Validation performed during this audit:

- `cargo check --no-default-features` passed on the current Windows host.
- `cargo check --no-default-features --features feat_windows` passed on the current Windows host.
- `cargo check -p rsac-napi` passed on the current Windows host.
- `git submodule status --recursive` did not list `apps/audio-graph`, confirming the stale `.gitmodules` entry is not represented by a committed gitlink in the current tree.

Worktree notes:

- No implementation files were intentionally edited by this audit.
- Existing dirty/untracked files were present or changed concurrently on the integration branch; preserve them unless a follow-up owns that scope.

closeReason: Non-actionable audit note indexing the now-deleted delegation-backlog doc; its areas are tracked by rsac-62bb + the CI matrix seeds. Triage w1kgqmn6s.

title: Epic: Multi-source channel compositor (compose feature)
description: In-crate, feature-gated (compose) multi-source capture composition. Groups of CaptureTargets; each group either mixes down (Mono/Stereo, per-source gain, plain summation, no auto-limiter) or keeps native channels; groups append in declaration order into ONE interleaved f32 multi-channel stream delivered via the existing CapturingStream contract (src/core/interface.rs:223) so drain_to/subscribe/async/WavFileSink work unchanged. Rate reconciliation: rubato resampling to session rate (default 48kHz) on the compositor thread (non-RT). Pacing: master-clock source (first system/device source) + per-source FIFOs, silence-pad behind / bounded-trim ahead, wall-clock fallback on master stall. New top DAG layer: core -> bridge -> audio -> api -> compose. Scope change vs VISION.md:140-169 (mixing non-goal) recorded via ADR. Timestamp-based drift correction deferred to rsac-ec25. Bindings (FFI/Python/Node) exposure deferred to a future epic.

title: Epic: Library UX + publish hygiene
description: Downstream-consumer UX and crates.io publish readiness: feature-gate CLI-only deps (clap/color-eyre/ctrlc/env_logger, Cargo.toml:85-90) behind a cli feature; fix repository URL mismatch (Cargo.toml:14 baladita vs Codeseys-Labs org used in README/AGENTS.md); exclude bindings/rsac-go from the crate tarball; enforce #![warn(missing_docs)] and fill the rustdoc gaps (ErrorKind variants, AudioError fields, audio::linux pub items); dedup the 3 identical CI badges in README.md.

title: Epic: CI hardening wave (MSRV, powerset, semver-checks, cleanup)
description: Close the remaining CI gaps found in the 2026-07 audit: no MSRV job (rust-version=1.87 declared but never built), no cargo-hack feature-powerset (single hand-picked combo on Linux only), no cargo-semver-checks release gate, stale blacksmith-audio-probe.yml workflow, fragile grep-based ARM64 pass/fail gates in ci.yml (cross-compile-linux-arm64, go-bindings-arm64-check). Feature-matrix checks become more important with the new compose + cli features (rsac-e336).

title: ADR: bring channel composition in-scope behind compose feature; amend VISION.md
description: Write a new MADR-format ADR in docs/designs/ (next number after existing 0001-000N) recording the deliberate scope change: VISION.md:140-169 currently declares stream mixing out-of-scope ('Why not own mixing?'). Decision: in-crate feature-gated compositor. Considered options (>=2 required): (a) in-crate compose feature [chosen], (b) separate rsac-mixer workspace crate (rejected: 7th manifest in version-lockstep CI, harder bindings exposure), (c) fan-in only per backlog M3 docs/reviews/rsac-ux-enhancement-backlog.md:146 (rejected: does not deliver channel grouping). Record the resolved sub-decisions: rubato resampling to session rate; master-clock pacing + silence-pad/bounded-trim + wall-clock fallback; group-based builder API; delivery via CapturingStream. Amend VISION.md out-of-scope table + 'Why not own mixing?' section to point at the compose feature; update AGENTS.md sections 2/5/7 where they assert mixing is downstream-only.

title: Compose scaffolding: feature flag, src/compose/ module, rubato dep, module-DAG update
description: Cargo.toml: add compose = ["dep:rubato"] to [features]; add rubato as optional dependency (verify the chosen version supports MSRV 1.87 per Cargo.toml:9 — check rubato rust-version before pinning; coordinate with the MSRV CI seed under rsac-b9df). Create src/compose/mod.rs (module docs) exported from src/lib.rs behind #[cfg(feature = "compose")]. Extend scripts/check-module-dag.sh: compose/ may import core/ and api/ (it consumes the public capture layer) but must never be imported by core/, bridge/, audio/, or api/ — add reverse-edge assertions + self-test case. cargo check --features compose green on Linux at minimum.

title: Compose types: CompositionBuilder, Group, GroupLayout, ChannelMap + preflight validation
description: src/compose/builder.rs: CompositionBuilder { sample_rate (default 48000, validated against PlatformCapabilities::SUPPORTED_SAMPLE_RATES like src/api.rs:65), group(Group) } -> build(). Group { name: String, sources: Vec<CaptureTarget>, per-source gain (default 1.0), layout: GroupLayout }. GroupLayout { Mono, Stereo, KeepChannels }. v1 restriction: KeepChannels groups require exactly ONE source — AudioError::ConfigurationError otherwise. ChannelMap: output channel index -> (group name, group-relative channel) for introspection; expose channel_map() on the built session. preflight(): at least 1 group, at least 1 source per group, unique group names, delegate per-target capability checks to the same PlatformCapabilities logic AudioCaptureBuilder::preflight uses (src/api.rs:284). Exhaustive unit tests for every validation error path.

title: Compose ingest + alignment engine: per-source FIFOs, master-clock pacing, silence-pad/trim, stats
description: src/compose/engine.rs: on start(), build + start one AudioCapture per source (the compositor OWNS consumption of every inner capture — document the single-consumer-per-ring rule; users must never read inner captures directly). Per-source sample FIFO (VecDeque<f32> or ring) filled from try_read_chunk on a dedicated compositor thread (named rsac-compose). Master clock = first source whose target is SystemDefault or Device (device clocks tick during silence; app taps do not), else the first source overall; output quantum follows the master buffer cadence. Sources behind the master at tick time are silence-padded; sources drifting ahead beyond a bounded FIFO depth are trimmed (bound configurable on the builder). If the master itself delivers nothing past a stall timeout, fall back to wall-clock ticks emitting silence so the session never freezes. Per-source counters (padded_frames, trimmed_frames, buffers_received) exposed via a CompositionStats struct. Teardown mirrors the CallbackPump/DrainHandle pattern (stop flag + join + self-join guard, src/api.rs:1142 and :843). Timestamp-based drift correction is explicitly OUT of scope here — deferred to rsac-ec25.

title: Compose resampling: rubato per-source to session rate
description: src/compose/resample.rs: per-source resampler for any source whose delivered rate differs from the session rate — critical on Windows where process loopback cannot use AUTOCONVERTPCM (src/audio/windows/wasapi.rs:152) and on Linux where the rate is only known after stream-open (query AudioCapture::format() after start(), src/api.rs:499 docs). Wrap rubato (evaluate SincFixedIn vs FftFixedIn for streaming chunks; document the latency/priming behavior). Deinterleave -> resample -> reinterleave on the compositor thread (non-RT, allocation acceptable). Unit tests: 44100->48000 and 48000->44100 sine round-trips with frequency-domain assertions (reuse the Goertzel helper approach from tests/ci_audio/helpers.rs).

title: Compose mixdown + channel composition + CapturingStream impl
description: src/compose/stream.rs: per-group mix at each tick: Mono = per-source mean across channels, then gain-weighted sum across sources (1 output channel); Stereo = mono sources duplicated to L/R, stereo pass-through, >2ch sources pairwise-folded (even->L odd->R, scaled), then gain-weighted sum (2 output channels); KeepChannels = single-source passthrough of native channels. Plain summation — NO automatic limiting/normalization (documented); optional saturating clamp flag on the builder, default off (f32 headroom preserved; WavFileSink f32 tolerates >1.0). Interleave groups in declaration order into the composed frame. Implement the CapturingStream contract (src/core/interface.rs:223): read_chunk/try_read_chunk (ADR-0003 terminal semantics: drain tail then StreamEnded), stop, format (session rate + total channels), is_running, counters, backpressure passthrough (aggregate inner captures), register_waker for async-stream. Public handle mirrors AudioCapture ergonomics (read_buffer, subscribe-style usage via drain_to where applicable) and exposes channel_map() + composition_stats().

title: Compose tests: mock-backend unit tests + ci_audio integration + example
description: Unit tests (no device): drive the compositor with the mock backend (src/bridge/mock.rs, 440Hz sine through the real BridgeStream pipeline) to deterministically verify mixdown math (mono mean, stereo fold, gain weighting), channel append order vs channel_map(), silence-padding + trimmed counters, KeepChannels single-source validation, and clamp flag. Integration: new tests/ci_audio/compose.rs module wired into tests/ci_audio/main.rs, gated behind cfg(feature = "compose") + require_audio!() helpers; on the Linux null-sink tier compose 2 sources (SystemDefault + spawned app via spawn_audio_player_get_pid) into a 3ch session, assert channel count/format hard and non-silence soft per the existing helpers.rs deterministic-env policy. Example: examples/composed_capture.rs + Cargo.toml [[example]] entry with required-features = ["compose"].

title: Compose docs: README section, rustdoc doctests, VISION Recently Shipped, ARCHITECTURE
description: README.md: new "Composing multiple sources" section with the group-based builder example, channel-map explanation, and the master-clock/silence-pad semantics; remove/adjust the comparison-table line saying mixing is out of scope. Rustdoc: module-level docs on src/compose/ with runnable no_run doctests (they execute in the feature-combo-doctests CI job once compose is wired there). VISION.md: move composition into the in-scope surface + Recently Shipped (coordinate with the ADR seed rsac-73a7 so prose stays consistent). docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: add the compose layer to the module-layering diagram.

title: CI: wire compose feature into lint/test/feature-combo/audio jobs
description: .github/workflows/ci.yml: include compose in the clippy --all-targets legs (it must stay -D warnings clean on all 3 platforms since rubato compiles everywhere); extend the feature-combo-doctests job combo to feat_linux,async-stream,sink-wav,test-utils,compose and run its doctests; confirm the docs job (--all-features + docsrs flags) renders compose items. .github/workflows/ci-audio-tests.yml: run the new compose integration module on the Linux tier (soft content asserts per existing policy). Keep the module-name filter strings in sync with the actual test module name (known filter-string fragility — add a wiring existence check like the macOS grep-based one in ci.yml test-macos).

title: Feature-gate CLI-only deps behind a cli feature
description: Cargo.toml: make clap, color-eyre, ctrlc, env_logger optional (dep:) behind a new cli feature (lines 85-90 today are unconditional — every library consumer pays for the CLI demo binary). Add required-features = ["cli"] to [[bin]] rsac and audit every src/bin/* target for which deps it actually uses (standardized_test, app_capture_test, pipewire_test, pipewire_diagnostics, wasapi_session_test, smoke_alpine) — extend their required-features accordingly. Decide + document whether cli joins default features (recommendation: NOT in defaults; document cargo install rsac --features cli in README). Verify with cargo tree that a --no-default-features --features feat_linux consumer no longer pulls clap/color-eyre/ctrlc/env_logger. Update CI legs that build --all-targets so bins still compile (they pass --all-features or add --features cli).

title: Publish hygiene: repository URL, exclude rsac-go from tarball, README badge dedup
description: Cargo.toml:14 repository points at github.com/baladita/rust-crossplat-audio-capture but README badges + AGENTS.md use the Codeseys-Labs org — set the canonical URL before any cargo publish. Add /bindings (or at minimum /bindings/rsac-go, which has no Cargo.toml and would be packaged) to the exclude list at Cargo.toml:18-43; verify with cargo package --list. README.md:36-38: the Linux/Windows/macOS unit-test rows embed the SAME ci.yml badge three times — replace with a single workflow badge or per-job badges. Note the Installation section (README:227, git-dependency only) needs updating at publish time.

title: Enforce #![warn(missing_docs)] and fill the rustdoc gaps
description: Add #![warn(missing_docs)] to src/lib.rs (currently only allow(clippy::result_large_err) + deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links) at lib.rs:1-2). Fill the known tail: ErrorKind variants (src/core/error.rs:34-40), AudioError variant fields (e.g. error.rs:140), platform-module pub items leaking through pub mod audio — enumerate_audio_applications() (src/audio/linux/mod.rs:100), LinuxDeviceEnumerator + new() (mod.rs:116-127), and whatever else the lint surfaces on all 3 platforms (run per-platform clippy legs; keep -D warnings green). Where an item should not be public API, prefer doc(hidden) or visibility reduction over boilerplate docs — but check semver impact first.

title: CI: MSRV (1.87) check job
description: .github/workflows/ci.yml: new job installing Rust 1.87.0 (dtolnay/rust-toolchain SHA-pinned like the rest) and running cargo check --all-features on blacksmith-4vcpu-ubuntu-2404 (Linux is sufficient as a floor guard; platform-specific deps are cfg-gated anyway — evaluate adding Windows/macOS legs only if cheap). Guards rust-version = "1.87" (Cargo.toml:9) from silently rotting under the pinned 1.95 toolchain. May need a -Zminimal-versions-free approach: just cargo check with the lockfile. NOTE: the compose feature adds rubato (rsac-07e3) — its MSRV must satisfy 1.87 or this job is the tripwire that catches it.

title: CI: cargo-hack feature-powerset check
description: .github/workflows/ci.yml: new Linux job using taiki-e/install-action (SHA-pinned) to get cargo-hack, then cargo hack check --feature-powerset --no-dev-deps --exclude-features feat_windows,feat_macos (platform features for other OSes cannot compile on Linux; alternatively use --group-features to collapse them). Covers the growing matrix: async-stream, sink-wav, test-utils, bridge-zerocopy, tracing, plus the new compose and cli features — today only ONE hand-picked combo is checked (feature-combo-doctests job). Watch job time; if the powerset explodes use --depth 2. Replaces/extends, not removes, the existing feature-combo-doctests job (that one also runs doctests).

title: Release: cargo-semver-checks gate in the verify stage
description: .github/workflows/release.yml: add a cargo-semver-checks step to the pre-publish verify stage (taiki-e/install-action or binstall, SHA-pinned). Until the crate exists on crates.io, run with --baseline-rev pointing at the previous release tag (release-tag.yml creates annotated vX.Y.Z tags); after first publish, default registry baseline works. Policy: hard-fail on breaking change without a major/minor bump per semver; document the gate + override procedure in docs/RELEASE_PROCESS.md. Run it for the root crate only (bindings have their own versioning lockstep already CI-enforced).

title: CI cleanup: delete stale audio-probe workflow; harden grep-based ARM64 gates
description: Delete .github/workflows/blacksmith-audio-probe.yml — its header says to remove it once probe findings are recorded, and they are (AGENTS.md section 6 runner table). Harden the fragile pass/fail gates in ci.yml cross-compile-linux-arm64 and go-bindings-arm64-check: they currently run the build with || true and then grep logs for error[ / error: — a rustc output format change or localized output could false-green. Replace with real exit-code handling: run cargo check --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu without || true where possible, or capture the exit code and only allow-list the specific known-acceptable failure (missing PipeWire system libs) by matching its exact diagnostic.

title: CI: PR-level device-free runtime smoke for napi + python bindings
description: check-bindings (ci.yml) is compile+clippy only for rsac-napi/rsac-python — their first RUNTIME execution is inside release-npm/release-pypi on the tag, so a PyO3/napi loader or ABI regression that compiles clean surfaces first during a release. Go already proves PR-level runtime smoke is cheap (make test-pure runs real code through cgo on 3 OSes). Minimal fix: extend the existing check-bindings Linux job with the SAME device-free smokes the release workflows already run — napi debug build + node -e require(...).platformCapabilities(), and a maturin-built wheel + the release-pypi import one-liner (import, platform_capabilities(), CaptureTarget.parse). Distinct from rsac-3635 (capture smoke, correctly deferred until a deterministic route exists).
closeReason: Delivered in 367847d (rsac-e1f7): check-bindings now maturin-builds + imports the Python wheel and napi-builds + node --tests the addon on every PR, device-free with headless-tolerant enumeration; CI-confirmed green on run 28763005463.

title: CI: drop continue-on-error from macOS audio jobs now that skip-gates exist
description: macos-system and macos-process in ci-audio-tests.yml are DOUBLE-soft: continue-on-error: true AND excluded from the summary gate — so even a panic or harness build break on macOS gates nothing. The workflow comment calls continue-on-error belt-and-braces until 1-2 green runs confirm the TCC skip-gate behaves; those runs exist once PR #35 is green. Removing continue-on-error converts macOS to at least panic/build-gated at zero infra cost (skip-gates + gtimeout belts already in place). Summary-gate inclusion can follow as a second step. Independent of (and interim to) the self-hosted TCC runner work in rsac-ad28. Also consider adding the armed enumeration_matrix step to macos-device while touching the file (mirrors the new windows-device step).

title: macOS: #[ignore] the hang-prone non-ignored tap.rs TCC tests (TC-03)
description: Original ask: #[ignore] the hang-prone non-ignored tap.rs TCC tests (TC-03). Audit 2026-07-16: the one test that genuinely hangs (test_new_system_creates_tap, calls AudioHardwareCreateProcessTap) is ALREADY #[ignore]d; the remaining 4 non-ignored tests did not hang locally on macOS 26/M4. Remaining scope: re-verify whether ci.yml's TC-03 comment is stale/overcautious and either ignore the remainder with evidence or update the comment and close.

title: CI: run the audio integration suite on release tags
description: ci-audio-tests.yml has no tags: trigger, so a release (v*.*.*) gates only on unit verify + semver-checks — the audio behavior of the published crate is vouched for solely by the last master-push run, which may predate the tag. Minimal fix: add the same stable-tag trigger ci.yml uses (v*.*.* with pre-release exclusions) to ci-audio-tests.yml; the summary gate already exists. Optionally make release.yml publish depend on it via a workflow_run check or keep it advisory-but-visible.

title: CI: execute criterion benches + run the ADR-0006 SampleRing promote-or-remove gate
description: No workflow runs cargo bench (grep: zero hits) — benches/bridge.rs and benches/observability.rs are compiled by --all-targets but never executed, so there is no throughput/latency regression signal (rt_alloc proves only the allocation property) AND the ADR-0006 bridge-zerocopy decision gate ("promote or remove based on A/B bench data by the next minor") can never fire. Minimal: a scheduled (cron) advisory Linux job running criterion quick mode with and without --features bridge-zerocopy, archiving baselines as artifacts. Then actually take the ADR-0006 branch: wire SampleRing into one interleaved-f32 backend and measure, or retire the feature. Paths: .github/workflows/ci.yml, benches/, docs/designs/0006-bridge-zerocopy-samplering.md. Overlaps AGENTS.md Phase-5 "performance benchmarking" (no prior seed).

title: First crates.io publish of rsac 0.4.x
description:
The crate is NOT on crates.io (API 404s) while README shows rsac = { version = "0.4" } and docs link docs.rs — broken promises until published. Git-only install blocks version pinning, cargo-semver-checks registry baselines, and future binding publishes referencing the crate. All automation exists (release-prepare.yml -> release-tag.yml -> release.yml dry-run+publish; docs.rs render gate in ci.yml). Blockers: set CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN secret, cut a v0.4.x tag via the release flow, verify cargo publish --dry-run, then flip README install snippet from git to version and verify docs.rs render + badges. Paths: .github/workflows/release.yml, Cargo.toml, README.md Installation.

---
2026-07-08 status: 0.4.1 released to GitHub - master 7956e6e (release: v0.4.1 #47), tag v0.4.1 + bindings/rsac-go/v0.4.1 pushed, GitHub Release published by release-tag.yml (run 28961891783). Publish path = GITHUB_TOKEN fallback (no release App secrets), so the registry publish workflows (crates.io/npm/PyPI) did NOT auto-fire. Remaining scope: configure CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN (+ npm/PyPI tokens, or the release GitHub App) and run the registry publishes against tag v0.4.1.

extensions.deferredReason: Owner decision 2026-07-08: push bindings and publishing back for now.

title: Wire tag->publish token and automate the bindings/rsac-go module tag
description: release-tag.yml pushes the vX.Y.Z tag with GITHUB_TOKEN, which deliberately never triggers release.yml/release-npm/release-pypi (GitHub anti-recursion) — the header documents that registry publishing is manual workflow_dispatch per registry. Fix: mint an installation token via actions/create-github-app-token (or a fine-grained PAT secret) for the tag push so the tag-triggered publish workflows fire. In the same job, also push the bindings/rsac-go/vX.Y.Z tag (RELEASE_PROCESS.md leaves it fully manual today — a forgotten tag means Go consumers cannot go get the release; nothing triggers on that tag path, so no recursion risk). Paths: .github/workflows/release-tag.yml, docs/RELEASE_PROCESS.md.

title: Composition consumption parity: subscribe() / audio_data_stream() / set_callback()
description: AudioCapture offers pull reads, push subscribe()/subscribe_with_errors(), callback delivery, and async audio_data_stream(); Composition (src/compose/stream.rs) has only pull reads + drain_to. The async case is egregious: register_waker is fully plumbed through ComposedStreamView but UNREACHABLE because AsyncAudioStream::new is pub(crate) and Composition exposes no audio_data_stream(). Push and async delivery are the primary consumption modes for real apps (VISION transcription use case). Reuse the existing subscribe-pump machinery from api.rs (spawn pump over the composed view Arc) and add audio_data_stream() returning AsyncAudioStream over &dyn CapturingStream. Paths: src/compose/stream.rs, src/bridge/async_stream.rs.

title: Compose: live per-source gain / mute on a running Composition
description: Per-source gains are frozen at Group::source_with_gain() build time; changing a level requires stop/rebuild/restart (drops audio). Any mixer/monitoring UI (e.g. apps/audio-graph) needs live control. Cheap RT-safe design: AtomicU32-encoded f32 gain (+ AtomicBool mute) per source in the shared stats/spec block, read by the compositor thread each tick (compositor is non-RT anyway); expose Composition::set_gain(group, source_idx, gain) / set_muted(...). Consider group-level master gain too. Paths: src/compose/{builder,engine,stream}.rs + tests in src/compose/tests.rs.

title: Expose compose through the Python/Node/Go bindings (C FFI surface already shipped)
description: STALE PREMISE CORRECTED (triage w1kgqmn6s): the C FFI compose surface ALREADY EXISTS — bindings/rsac-ffi/src/compose.rs ships ~30 rsac_composition_* fns (170 refs) with headers regenerated (rsac.h/rsac_generated.h), landed via the 0.4.1 stack (commit 5e38c3c). REMAINING: the higher-language wrappers — Python (PyO3), Node (napi), Go (cgo) all have ZERO compose refs in src. Mechanical: wrap the existing opaque-handle C surface (create/group-add/mixdown/keep-channels/build/start/read/stats/channel-map/stop/free) per binding, gated on the compose feature in each binding crate's Cargo.toml (note rsac-2938: node feature selection uses root defaults). Wide but mechanical (L).

title: Close the pipe_to story: drain_to on plain AudioCapture + purge stale mentions
description: README and docs/API.md tell users there is no pipe_to() driver and to hand-roll loops, but drain_to (the actual driver: background thread, recoverable-vs-fatal policy, flush/close finalization) exists on RunningCapture and Composition — NOT on a plain started AudioCapture (the &mut start() path). Add AudioCapture::drain_to() delegating to the shared spawn_drain_thread (src/api.rs — trivial), declare drain_to THE answer, and update README:65, VISION.md roadmap bullet, docs/API.md Sinks section, docs/architecture/API_DESIGN.md tracked-items list. Decide whether pipe_to becomes an alias or the name is retired.

title: Compose v2 layouts: multi-source KeepChannels + surround (5.1) group layouts

title: Android AudioPlaybackCapture backend: Kotlin AudioRecord loop + JNI ingest (SystemDefault/Application/ApplicationByName/ProcessTree)
description:
The Java-only path per docs/MOBILE_BACKEND_DESIGN.md + ADR-0013: src/audio/android/{playback.rs,jni.rs} orchestrating the AAR's capture service (rsac-c4b8). Rust registers nativePush via RegisterNatives; the AAR's dedicated Java thread loops AudioRecord.read(float[]) and calls nativePush(session, buf, frames, channels, rate); Rust copies via GetFloatArrayRegion into a session-lifetime scratch buffer and push_samples_or_drop()s (no per-call allocation on the Rust side - adapted ADR-0001 statement in the design doc; ring-full => drop + overrun count, never block). Target mapping is normative from ADR-0013: SystemDefault = usage filters no UID; Application = addMatchingUid; ApplicationByName = package->UID via AAR helper; ProcessTree = PID->UID via /proc/<pid>/status (tree==app equivalence documented). Token lifecycle: consumed from with_android_projection (rsac-82d4), released (DeleteGlobalRef + MediaProjection.stop()) on capture drop.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo ndk check passes; JNI symbol names match the AAR's CaptureBridge declarations (CI greps both sides or a shared codegen guard)
- Terminal semantics: service stop / projection revoked => producer terminal signal (ADR-0010) => fatal terminal (ADR-0003)

- Capture-matrix + capabilities updated: all four playback tiers true on SDK>=29 with consent

title: Replace the undocumented .cargo/config.toml v0_3_65 rustflags hack with a real feature declaration
description:

Verify Linux CI (which actually compiles pipewire) stays green and the ARM64 cross jobs behave identically.

title: Compose engine panic leaves composition permanently non-terminal
description: Engine::run (src/compose/engine.rs:210-288) is spawned bare (stream.rs:518); teardown (reader stops, producer.signal_done, active=false) only runs on clean loop exit. A panic in the tick loop (indexing, mixdown slice math, resampler invariant) skips all of it: ring state stays Running, engine_active stays true, so ComposedStreamView::read_chunk loops on swallowed Timeouts forever, subscribe/drain pumps spin at 1ms forever, rsac_composition_read blocks a C caller indefinitely, is_running lies, and Composition::stop joins and silently discards the panic. Found independently by 3 reviewers. Fix: catch_unwind/drop-guard around the loop with an infallible teardown that signals signal_error() on panic (signal_done on clean exit) + active=false; log the join error in stop(). Acceptance: a test with a panicking scripted source/reader observes a terminal error from read_buffer_blocking within the stall timeout, and is_running returns false. (effort: S | verify: cargo test --lib --features compose | wave: 1)
closeReason: Fixed in the review batch on work/critique-ci-audio-integration (commits between the compose panic-containment commit and the FFI completeness commit); validated: 640 lib tests (compose,async-stream), 40 FFI tests, rt_alloc 4 proofs, gate green

title: FFI: enum-by-value params are UB for out-of-range ints (rsac_group_set_layout + rsac_default_device)
description: rsac_group_set_layout (bindings/rsac-ffi/src/compose.rs:200) takes rsac_group_layout_t, a fieldless repr(C) Rust enum, BY VALUE. Any int outside {0,1,2} from C (uninitialized var, future constant) materializes an invalid Rust enum value at the ABI boundary - immediate UB before the null check or match runs. Pre-existing twin: rsac_default_device kind: rsac_device_kind_t (lib.rs:1262). Fix: take i32 (validate + map to the Rust enum, RSAC_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER on out-of-range); keep the C header enum for constants, verify cbindgen emits int32_t param and update curated header to match. Acceptance: both functions take a validated integer; a device-free test passes an out-of-range value and gets INVALID_PARAMETER; header drift check passes. (effort: S | verify: cargo test -p rsac-ffi --features compose && header drift check | wave: 1)
closeReason: Fixed in the review batch on work/critique-ci-audio-integration (commits between the compose panic-containment commit and the FFI completeness commit); validated: 640 lib tests (compose,async-stream), 40 FFI tests, rt_alloc 4 proofs, gate green

title: FFI: rsac_composition_builder_add_group consumes the group before fallible work - double-free on panic path
description: compose.rs:379-381 does Box::from_raw(group) BEFORE builder.inner.clone().group(...) which can panic (alloc). On panic the unwind drops the group (freed) while catch returns RSAC_ERROR_PANIC - but the documented contract (compose.rs:359, both headers) says on any error the caller still owns the group and must free it -> double-free. Fix: borrow the group, clone its inner through the fallible work, and only Box::from_raw+drop after success (build keeps its always-consumes contract, which is correct as documented). Acceptance: reordered so no fallible call sits between from_raw and success return; ownership rustdoc unchanged and now true. (effort: XS | verify: cargo test -p rsac-ffi --features compose | wave: 1)
closeReason: Fixed in the review batch on work/critique-ci-audio-integration (commits between the compose panic-containment commit and the FFI completeness commit); validated: 640 lib tests (compose,async-stream), 40 FFI tests, rt_alloc 4 proofs, gate green

title: Subscribe pumps use unbounded mpsc - unbounded memory growth that also defeats overrun accounting
description: spawn_subscribe_thread / spawn_subscribe_with_errors_thread (src/api.rs:868,915) use mpsc::channel() (unbounded). A consumer slower than real time grows the channel without bound; worse, the pump drains the ring promptly so overrun_count()/is_under_backpressure()/backpressure_report() all read healthy while memory climbs - violating the crate-wide drop-dont-block-and-count-it policy. Affects AudioCapture AND Composition (shared helpers). Fix: sync_channel(N) + try_send; on Full, drop the buffer and count it (subscriber-side drop counter surfaced via the handle or the shared overrun counter); coalesce repeated recoverable errors in the with-errors pump; document the bound + drop policy on all four subscribe methods. Acceptance: a stalled receiver caps channel depth at N, drops are counted and observable, docs updated, existing subscribe tests still pass. (effort: M | verify: cargo test --lib --features compose,async-stream | wave: 2)
closeReason: Fixed in the review batch on work/critique-ci-audio-integration (commits between the compose panic-containment commit and the FFI completeness commit); validated: 640 lib tests (compose,async-stream), 40 FFI tests, rt_alloc 4 proofs, gate green

title: WASAPI capture-thread panic skips the terminal signal - readers spin forever
description: wasapi_capture_thread_main (src/audio/windows/thread.rs) has no unwind protection: a panic anywhere in the loop unwinds past the cleanup tail (:716-745), so is_active stays true, neither signal_done nor signal_error fires, and a blocked read_chunk degrades to infinite Timeout-retry instead of fatal StreamEnded - violating the ADR-0010 cross-backend contract that no exit path leaves the bridge non-terminal. Fix: drop-guard owning is_active + a producer handle whose Drop does is_active.store(false) + signal_error() unless disarmed by the clean-exit path (or catch_unwind routing into the existing fatal_error tail). Acceptance: guard in place, clean path disarms, existing Windows lib tests pass. (effort: S | verify: cargo test --lib --features feat_windows | wave: 1)
closeReason: Fixed in the review batch on work/critique-ci-audio-integration (commits between the compose panic-containment commit and the FFI completeness commit); validated: 640 lib tests (compose,async-stream), 40 FFI tests, rt_alloc 4 proofs, gate green

title: Timestamps: survive mid-stream rate renegotiation and centralize the frames_offered advance
description: Two related soft spots in the stamped push design. (1) push_samples_or_drop_stamped computes frames_offered/current_rate (ring_buffer.rs:743-746); on Linux param_changed can update sample_rate mid-stream (linux/thread.rs:2040), retroactively rescaling the whole timeline (48k->44.1k after 10s = spurious ~884ms jump, corrupting the documented gap contract). Fix: accumulate position_nanos (u64) instead - stamp = position_nanos, then advance by frames*1e9/rate via u128; truncation <1ns/call, rate changes become seamless. (2) Only the _stamped variants advance the counter; mixing stamped and unstamped pushes on one producer silently desyncs the timeline (negative gaps). Fix: move the advance into push_samples_or_drop_inner so every offered buffer advances it; untimed variants just do not read it. Add unit tests: rate-change mid-stream continuity, mixed-variant contiguity, multiple consecutive drops. Keep rt_alloc guarantees (pure integer math only). Acceptance: both fixes in, new tests pass, rt_alloc suite still 0-alloc. (effort: M | verify: cargo test --lib && cargo test --test rt_alloc -- --test-threads=1 | wave: 1)
closeReason: Fixed in the review batch on work/critique-ci-audio-integration (commits between the compose panic-containment commit and the FFI completeness commit); validated: 640 lib tests (compose,async-stream), 40 FFI tests, rt_alloc 4 proofs, gate green

title: PipeWire .process: log::warn + debug_assert on the RT thread outside the panic guard
description: linux/thread.rs:2185-2194 formats (allocates) and typically locks+syscalls via log::warn! inside the .process C callback, outside catch_unwind - a panicking/allocating logger unwinds across PipeWire C frames (UB class PS-4). The callback doc comment (:2078) even claims no logging. Also the debug_assert at :2175-2179 is a panic site outside the guard in debug builds. Fix: set an AtomicBool + truncated-bytes counter in the callback; emit the log from the non-RT side (stop path or consumer), mirroring the WASAPI diagnostics pattern; drop or relocate the debug_assert. Acceptance: no logging/panic sites in the .process callback outside the guard; one-shot diagnostic still surfaces from the non-RT side; feat_linux check passes. (effort: S | verify: cargo check --features feat_linux && cargo test --lib | wave: 1)
closeReason: Fixed in the review batch on work/critique-ci-audio-integration (commits between the compose panic-containment commit and the FFI completeness commit); validated: 640 lib tests (compose,async-stream), 40 FFI tests, rt_alloc 4 proofs, gate green

title: Compose: resampled sources lose their tail at natural end - StreamResampler has no flush
description: StreamResampler::push only processes full 1024-frame chunks (resample.rs:79-84); when a source ends, pending (up to 1023 frames, ~21ms at 48k) is abandoned AND the FFT output_delay residue (deliberately swallowed at the front, resample.rs:117-119) never comes out - ~25-45ms lost per resampled source, violating flush_tail's stated no-audio-discarded contract (engine.rs:423-424). Fix: add StreamResampler::flush - process the final partial chunk via rubato v3 Indexing partial_len, then one all-zero chunk to expel output_delay, trim to expected frame count; call from ingest_all before marking ended. Tighten the scripted test tolerance (currently within-3-chunks at tests.rs:454-461, which tolerates the bug) to +/-1 frame of input*to/from. (effort: M | verify: cargo test --lib --features compose | wave: 1)
closeReason: Fixed in the review batch on work/critique-ci-audio-integration (commits between the compose panic-containment commit and the FFI completeness commit); validated: 640 lib tests (compose,async-stream), 40 FFI tests, rt_alloc 4 proofs, gate green

title: Compose: intra-source gap compensation from buffer timestamps + fix stale no-timestamps docs
description: The engine ignores buffer.timestamp() on ingest (engine.rs:343-405). When an inner capture ring overflows, the composed output time-compresses that source (post-gap audio splices onto pre-gap) - a permanent cross-source desync the pad/trim model cannot see. The stamped-push drop-gap semantics were built for exactly this consumer. Fix (no device clocks needed): per source track expected_next; if ts > expected_next + epsilon, insert (ts-expected_next)*rate frames of silence (bounded by max_fifo_frames), count in a new gap_padded_frames stat, update expected_next. Also fix the now-FALSE claims: src/compose/mod.rs:39-40 (no backend populates timestamp yet) and ADR-0011 section 4.3 blocked-on note (amend with a status addendum, do not rewrite). Also surface inner-ring drops: add SourceReader::overruns() forwarding capture.overrun_count() into SourceStats. Acceptance: scripted test with a gapped source shows silence insertion + stat; docs amended. (effort: M | verify: cargo test --lib --features compose | wave: 1)
closeReason: Fixed in the review batch on work/critique-ci-audio-integration (commits between the compose panic-containment commit and the FFI completeness commit); validated: 640 lib tests (compose,async-stream), 40 FFI tests, rt_alloc 4 proofs, gate green

title: Compose engine: use stamped push + notify_consumers; truncate partial frames on ingest
description: Three engine-loop upgrades. (1) emit_tick (engine.rs:495-506) hand-rolls AudioBuffer::with_timestamp(out.to_vec()) - one heap alloc per tick forever - and a manual frames_emitted counter; push_samples_or_drop_stamped is semantically identical and free-list backed (alloc-free steady state). (2) The engine never calls producer.notify_consumers() after pushes - the compositor is non-RT, exactly the case that API exists for; without it blocking readers wake only via the 1ms backstop poll. (3) ingest extends the FIFO with raw samples without guaranteeing samples.len() %% channels == 0 (engine.rs:373-375); one dangling half-frame rotates channel interleave forever after - truncate to whole frames with a one-shot warn. Acceptance: no per-tick alloc in steady state, notify called after push, partial-frame test added. (effort: S | verify: cargo test --lib --features compose | wave: 1)
closeReason: Fixed in the review batch on work/critique-ci-audio-integration (commits between the compose panic-containment commit and the FFI completeness commit); validated: 640 lib tests (compose,async-stream), 40 FFI tests, rt_alloc 4 proofs, gate green

title: Go module tag fan-out cannot work: module path does not match the tagged repo
description: bindings/rsac-go/go.mod declares module github.com/Codeseys-Labs/rsac-go but release-tag.yml pushes bindings/rsac-go/vX.Y.Z to rust-crossplat-audio-capture. Go subdir-tag convention only applies when the module path is repo-path-prefixed, so go get ...@vX.Y.Z can never resolve; the step succeeds but achieves nothing, and the CHANGELOG/RELEASE_PROCESS claims are false as wired. Fix: change go.mod to module github.com/Codeseys-Labs/rust-crossplat-audio-capture/bindings/rsac-go (update internal imports, Makefile, README, INTEROP references) OR document+implement the mirror-repo tagging path; correct CHANGELOG + RELEASE_PROCESS claims to match reality. Acceptance: a go get against the declared module path can actually resolve once a tag exists (verified via go mod download resolution logic / documented), docs truthful. (effort: M | verify: go build ./... in bindings/rsac-go + doc review | wave: 1)

title: CI/release gaps: compose FFI never compiled in CI; npm publish flow; release.yml dispatch guard; SHA pinning; async-stream platform legs
description: Five workflow gaps from review. (1) The 24 compose FFI functions are never compiled in CI: check-bindings runs cargo check/clippy -p rsac-ffi WITHOUT --features compose (ci.yml:441,451) and the header drift step builds without it - add compose legs to check, clippy, and drift check. (2) release-npm.yml: prepublishOnly (napi prepublish) runs in a step without NODE_AUTH_TOKEN then the final publish re-triggers it -> auth failure or E403 double-publish; restructure (drop explicit prepublish + token on publish step, or --ignore-scripts). title: Consumption API preconditions and naming: Stopping-window subscribe, drain_to gate parity, stop/start contract, Composition read_buffer naming, async waker clobber
description: Precondition/naming divergences between AudioCapture and Composition found by review. (1) subscribe/subscribe_with_errors gate on is_running(), refusing to attach during the drainable Stopping window - a fast-ending composition can strand its entire output; use a readability check (Running||Stopping) or drop the gate. (2) Composition::drain_to skips the is_running precondition RunningCapture::drain_to enforces while claiming same policy - pick a side for both. (3) AudioCapture stop() then start() silently re-creates a stream while stop() rustdoc says cannot be restarted - bless-and-document or reject; Composition rejects, so the handles disagree; also fix the misleading post-stop subscribe error message. (4) Composition::read_buffer{,_blocking} are terminal-observable while AudioCapture methods of the SAME NAME are the downgraded family - rename Composition's to read_chunk_nonblocking/read_chunk_blocking style (compose is unreleased; cheap now, expensive after 1.0) and update FFI call sites + docs. (5) Two concurrent AsyncAudioStreams clobber the single AtomicWaker - second task can park forever; wake-by-ref displaced waker on replacement and/or document single-async-consumer. (6) Re-export DrainHandle (+AudioBufferIterator) at crate root/prelude. Add tests: Composition subscribe buffers-only pump, subscribe-after-stop, stop-then-start pinning. title: Compose FFI completeness: overrun visibility, builder knobs, non-consuming preflight, threading contract doc
description: Surface gaps keeping the compose C API from complete. title: Package librsac.so for Android: cdylib build via cargo-ndk + AAR jniLibs wiring
description:
Prerequisite for rsac-77f1's JNI symbols actually loading: the rsac crate currently builds rlib only - Android needs a cdylib (librsac.so) per ABI. Decide the mechanism (add crate-type cdylib to the root crate vs a thin rsac-android wrapper crate that re-exports and forces cdylib - wrapper avoids penalizing desktop consumers with cdylib artifacts), build via cargo-ndk for arm64-v8a (+x86_64 for emulator), and wire the output into mobile/android's jniLibs so gradle assembleRelease packages it. Extend the mobile-android CI job to build the .so and assert it lands inside the AAR. RsacProjection.isNativeAvailable() flips to true from the packaged AAR.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo-ndk produces librsac.so for arm64-v8a and x86_64 in CI
- AAR contains jni/<abi>/librsac.so (assertion in the mobile-android job)
- Decision (root cdylib vs wrapper crate) recorded in the commit/doc
- Desktop builds unaffected (no cdylib in default desktop artifacts if wrapper chosen)

(effort: medium | verify: android-ci | wave: 3)

title: Compose: flush the old resampler before rebuilding on mid-stream rate change
description: CONVERGENT finding (Codex P2 + CodeRabbit Major, PR #36 engine.rs:558/:565): when an already-resampled source renegotiates its delivered rate, ingest replaces s.resampler with a fresh StreamResampler - the old one still holds a partial input chunk + FFT output_delay residue (~25-45ms), which is silently dropped. The natural-end path flushes (rsac-fab0); the rate-CHANGE path does not. Fix: call the existing flush() on the old resampler and push its tail into the FIFO before constructing the replacement; scripted test with a mid-stream rate change asserting no lost frames. (effort: S | verify: cargo test --lib --features compose | wave: post-merge-1)
closeReason: Fixed in PR #41 (squash-merged to release/0.4.1): src/compose/engine.rs flushes the old resampler into the source FIFO before rebuilding on a mid-stream rate change, mirroring the rsac-fab0 natural-end pattern. Regression test mid_stream_rate_change_flushes_old_resampler_tail proves the bug (8320/9600 frames without the fix - 1280-frame tail dropped) and exactness with it (tol 2). 633 lib tests green; gate-equivalent clippy clean. Codex P2 review finding on the adjacent direct-rate path tracked as rsac-b7d4. Convergent finding origin: Codex P2 + CodeRabbit Major on PR #36.

description: Adjacent finding from the rsac-7d97 fix (PR #41): when an already-resampled source renegotiates its delivered rate to exactly the SESSION rate, ingest takes the direct (no-resampler) path at src/compose/engine.rs:534 and the old resampler object is left holding its tail (pending partial chunk + output_delay residue). The tail is only recovered later by the natural-end flush - i.e. delivered OUT OF ORDER after newer direct-path frames (not lost, but misordered). Fix if the transition is considered reachable: flush the old resampler into the FIFO before taking the direct path, mirroring the rate-change fix. (effort: S | verify: cargo test --lib --features compose with a scripted rate->session-rate transition | wave: post-merge-1)

title: Windows: per-PID Application capture never delivers content on windows-latest while ProcessTree loopback of the same player captures the tone
description: Evidence from ci-audio-tests runs 28902137869 (work/0.4.1-win-e2e) + historical release-branch runs: test_app_capture_by_process_id (CaptureTarget::Application(pid) of the spawned PlayLooping powershell player) reads buffers but ALL silence-padded (Max amplitude 0.000000 for the full 15 s window), on every recorded run - while test_process_tree_capture_receives_audio (CaptureTarget::ProcessTree(pid), SAME player mechanism, same endpoint, same run) captures the tone at RMS about 0.53. So the WASAPI per-PID session-capture path (G1) fails to resolve/attach the player's audio session by PID on windows-latest, while the process-loopback path (G2) works. Investigate: how does the Windows backend resolve Application(pid) (IAudioSessionManager2 enumeration timing? session created after enumeration? session PID attribution for winmm/PlaySound?) and either fix the resolution or route Application(pid) through process loopback include-tree (which on Windows would make app == tree semantics - if chosen, record as an ADR note). Until fixed, the windows-process CI job's app_capture step carries a documented deterministic-mode opt-out. Acceptance: test_app_capture_by_process_id passes with RSAC_CI_AUDIO_DETERMINISTIC=1 on windows-latest for 2+ consecutive runs; remove the step-level opt-out. (effort: M | verify: ci-audio-tests windows-process job | wave: post-merge-1)

title: Bindings: Python/napi blocking reads use read_buffer_blocking - inherits the documented terminal-downgrade race; migrate to read_chunk_blocking
description: Found during the rsac-b106 evidence loop (run 28908418725 analysis): rsac-python (src/lib.rs:1036) and rsac-napi (src/lib.rs:457,507) implement their blocking reads over AudioCapture::read_buffer_blocking, whose is_running() compatibility guard only surfaces the fatal StreamEnded when the terminal transition lands DURING an already-parked read (documented on the method; pinned by api.rs unit tests). A binding consumer that calls request_stop and then keeps reading can spin on recoverable 'not running' errors forever instead of observing StreamEnded - exactly the ADR-0003 busy-wait the terminal contract exists to kill, and deterministic on data-flowing streams (the Linux lifecycle test hit this: tests/ci_audio/lifecycle_terminal.rs docs carry the evidence trail). Fix: migrate both bindings to read_chunk_blocking (the terminal-preserving surface rsac-ffi/C/Go already get) and add a binding-level unblock test. (effort: S | verify: python/napi smokes + a request_stop-unblock test per binding | wave: post-merge-1)
closeReason: Fixed in master 3650456 (PR #54): python read() + napi readBlocking/readBlockingAsync migrated to read_chunk_blocking; docs updated in lib.rs/index.d.ts/__init__.pyi; behavior change under CHANGELOG Changed. Post-stop verified via maturin develop + node smokes.

title: macOS: list_audio_applications() cannot signal filtered-vs-fallback mode - callers can't tell audio-producers from all-GUI-apps
description: Surfaced by the codex-bot review on PR #45: enumerate_audio_applications() (macOS) silently falls back to the FULL unfiltered NSWorkspace list when the CoreAudio audio-process query is unavailable or finds no active producers (coreaudio.rs:186-197) — deliberate honest-fallback, but INVISIBLE to callers: list_audio_applications()'s rustdoc says 'applications that may be producing audio' with no mode indicator, so a consumer building an app-picker (audio-graph) or a test discovering a target cannot distinguish 'these 2 apps are producing audio' from 'here are all 131 GUI apps'. The PR #45 test fix had to resort to heuristics (len>10 or Finder-present). Options: (a) return an enum/struct with a mode flag {Filtered, UnfilteredFallback}, (b) add list_audio_applications_filtered() -> Option<Vec<...>> that returns None in fallback mode, (c) at minimum document the fallback in the public rustdoc (currently absent — docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:194 mentions the superset for Win/Linux but not the macOS fallback). API design decision; coordinate with rsac-86ee (CLI list-apps) which would surface the same ambiguity to users. Evidence: tests/ci_audio/application_by_name.rs:56-76 (the heuristic workaround), verified live: 2-app filtered list with Firefox playing vs 131-app fallback with audio stopped.

title: Windows compose E2E: composed_system_capture flakes when the PlayLooping player cold-starts slower than the capture window
description: Observed on PR #44 run 28913508593 (windows-system, deterministic): compose::composed_system_capture_layout_and_delivery panicked at the nonsilent assert with engine stats ticks=164 fallback_ticks=164 - the WASAPI loopback source delivered ZERO packets for the whole ~2s composed window, i.e. the test's own tone player (powershell SoundPlayer PlayLooping, 1-3s cold start) was not rendering yet, so event-driven loopback of the idle endpoint produced nothing and the composition was pure fallback silence. The plain system_capture tests in the SAME step passed (their player warmup sufficed). Same code passed twice consecutively on dispatch runs 28904467217/28905109235 - timing flake, not a regression. Fix ideas: (a) in the composed test, block until the player is audibly rendering (reuse the plain tests' first-nonsilent wait) before starting the composed read window; (b) lengthen the composed window; (c) share one warm player across the step. Acceptance: 3 consecutive green windows-system runs with the fix; no soft-skip added under deterministic env. (effort: S | verify: ci-audio-tests windows-system x3 | wave: post-merge-1)
closeReason: Fixed in master 3650456 (PR #54): bounded 8s warm-up nonsilence poll before the strict 2s window, with layout asserts on warm-up buffers; Windows CI compose leg green on the PR.

title: AndroidProjectionToken is Copy/Clone - safe Rust can create duplicate owners of one JNI GlobalRef
description:
Codex P1 on PR #47 (release 0.4.1 -> master), review comment 3541337631 on src/core/config.rs:105.

AndroidProjectionToken derives Copy/Clone, but on Android playback captures the token's raw i64 is a JNI GlobalRef that AndroidPlaybackStream::stop_and_close stops and deletes. Safe callers can duplicate the owner (reuse the token across two builds, or clone an AudioCaptureBuilder after with_android_projection); two streams then hold the same jobject, the first teardown deletes it, and the second stream uses/deletes a stale GlobalRef (JNI-level UB). The rustdoc's 'stale value is not UB at this layer' claim does not hold for the double-delete path.

Fix direction (design, not a one-liner): make the token single-use/non-Copy (consume it in with_android_projection / build), or explicitly retain-on-clone (NewGlobalRef per duplicate). Ripples into StreamConfig/builder Clone derives and the FFI int64_t carry - the C surface can stay i64 with the single-owner contract documented, but the Rust surface must enforce it. Update the AndroidProjectionToken rustdoc claim either way.

Deferred from the release PR per the identity-preserving policy: mobile is compile-only/unverified, and this is an API-shaping change. Must land before (or with) Android runtime verification - rsac-e6d3 claims depend on teardown being sound.

(effort: M | verify: cargo check+clippy --target aarch64-linux-android --features feat_android; a compile-fail or API test proving a token cannot be used twice | wave: post-0.4.1-merge-1)

title: gitattributes: pin rsac_generated.h (and generated artifacts) to LF to stop local EOL churn
description:
On Windows, any local build of rsac-ffi regenerates bindings/rsac-ffi/include/rsac_generated.h with LF while git's autocrlf marks the file dirty (content-identical, 0-line diff) - it blocked a branch switch and needed manual 'git restore' four separate times during the 0.4.1 release session. Add a .gitattributes rule (e.g. '*.h -text' scoped to the include dir, or 'bindings/rsac-ffi/include/* text eol=lf') so generated artifacts are EOL-stable across platforms; audit for other generated files with the same churn.

title: Lock and harden npm binding dependency installation
description: Finding: rsac-napi uses semver-ranged @napi-rs/cli without a committed lockfile, release-npm uses floating Bun and falls back from frozen-lockfile to an unfrozen install. Acceptance: commit and enforce an npm or bun lockfile, stop ignoring package-lock if that lockfile is chosen, pin Bun or use npm ci, and remove unfrozen release fallbacks. Verify: deleting or modifying the lockfile fails CI/release install; napi build and smoke still pass.

title: Add non-Rust supply-chain audit gates
description: Finding: cargo-deny covers Rust dependencies, but npm, Python, Gradle/Kotlin, Go, and future SwiftPM dependencies are not audited or license-gated in CI. Acceptance: add explicit advisory and license checks for npm, Python, Gradle/Kotlin, and Go, with a documented SwiftPM guard if external Swift dependencies appear. Verify: CI exposes separate audit jobs and a seeded vulnerable or disallowed test dependency fails the relevant ecosystem gate.

title: Remove package-manager execution from Cargo build script
description: Finding: build.rs can run sudo apt-get update/install when RSAC_AUTO_INSTALL=1, and scripts/setup_env.sh exports that variable. This is surprising for a library build and weakens reproducibility. Acceptance: build.rs never invokes sudo, apt-get, yum, brew, or other package managers; setup scripts install dependencies explicitly; missing PipeWire dev packages produce a clear diagnostic with a docs link. Verify: grep build.rs for package-manager commands and run a Linux feat_linux check in an environment without PipeWire dev headers to confirm clean failure guidance.

title: Harden release reproducibility and artifact provenance
description: Finding: release jobs install mutable OS packages from live repositories, cargo publish lacks --locked, cargo-deny allows yanked crates as warnings, and release artifacts do not have a complete SBOM/provenance story. Acceptance: publish and dry-run use --locked, yanked crates are denied or explicitly justified, OS package versions are pinned or recorded, and release artifacts emit SBOM or provenance artifacts where practical. Verify: release dry-run logs locked dependency resolution, package versions, and provenance/SBOM artifacts; a yanked test dependency fails policy in a controlled branch.

title: Add mobile consent parity to FFI and Go bindings
description: Finding: Rust exposes Android projection and iOS App Group consent configuration, C FFI exposes only the Android projection setter, and Go does not project the mobile consent setters despite claiming header/API lockstep. Acceptance: C FFI exposes iOS App Group configuration or explicitly documents it unsupported; Go exposes Android projection and iOS App Group builder methods or documents mobile binding limits; header mirror lockstep catches drift. Verify: cargo test -p rsac-ffi, Go pure tests, and mobile cross-check compile legs pass.

title: Reconcile iOS ReplayKit Darwin notification contract with Rust consumer behavior
description: Finding: Swift RingLayout and SampleHandlerTemplate describe Darwin notifications as part of the host/Rust consumer contract, while the Rust ReplayKit consumer appears to use bounded polling plus heartbeat staleness rather than listening for Darwin notifications. Acceptance: either implement/verify a Darwin notification listener in the Rust host path or update Swift/docs/ADR text so the contract is heartbeat-poll-only; terminal semantics remain tested. Verify: contract test or code/doc grep proves Swift and Rust agree on start/stop/liveness signaling.

title: macOS: Device(input-only) capture fails with OSStatus -10851 — CurrentDevice set before EnableIO (TN2091 ordering)
description:
Mirrors GitHub issue #53 (seed is source of truth; GH issue is the public report).

CaptureTarget::Device(<input-only device>) fails at AudioCapture::start() with OSStatus -10851 (kAudioUnitErr_InvalidPropertyValue) for EVERY input-only device, i.e. every actual microphone. Root cause: create_macos_capture (src/audio/macos/thread.rs:333-406) sets kAudioOutputUnitProperty_CurrentDevice BEFORE kAudioOutputUnitProperty_EnableIO, so the AUHAL's still-enabled output bus tries to bind to a device with no output streams and rejects it. Combo input+output devices (USB headsets) mask the bug because their output element binds fine — which is why dev machines never saw it.

Fix per Apple TN2091: enable input / disable output FIRST, then set CurrentDevice. GH #53 contains a step-by-step OSStatus replay proving the reordered sequence passes on the same device (MacBook Air Microphone, device 84) where the current order fails on the first call.

Repro'd on rsac v0.4.1 (7956e6ef), macOS 26 arm64, via public API from both a terminal binary and a signed .app — instant failure, pre-TCC (permission problems present as silent zeros/hang, never -10851).
closeReason: Fixed in master 3650456 (PR #54): TN2091 reorder in create_macos_capture, AUHAL_SETUP_ORDER + order-pinning unit test. Hardware-verified on M4: pre-fix master fails -10851 on both input-only devices, fixed branch passes 2/2.

title: macOS device-watch: race-free listener teardown via AudioObjectAddPropertyListenerBlock on self-owned serial queue
description:
Mirrors GitHub issue #32 (seed is source of truth; this is the tracking issue promised by closed seed rsac-63e5 / ADR-0005 §5).

The shipped Wave-B watch-UAF fix INTENTIONALLY LEAKS the WatchListenerContext at teardown because AudioObjectRemovePropertyListener gives no guarantee an in-flight watch_listener_proc has finished (PROC-based CoreAudio listeners have no barrier). Proper race-free fix: switch to the block-based listener API (AudioObjectAddPropertyListenerBlock) on a self-owned serial dispatch queue, remove on the same queue so no callback can be in flight after teardown — then the context can be freed instead of leaked.

Files: src/audio/macos/coreaudio.rs watch()/teardown. See ADR-0005 §5.

title: Bindings: wrapper mutex held across parked blocking reads can deadlock stop()
description:
From CodeRabbit review of PR #54 (rsac-477d migration). Both bindings hold their wrapper mutex (napi: self.inner Mutex, python: self.inner Mutex with GIL released) while parked inside a blocking read (read_chunk_blocking, and previously read_buffer_blocking - the exposure is PRE-EXISTING on v0.4.1, not introduced by the migration: read_buffer_blocking's is_running() guard runs before parking, so a running-but-silent stream parks with the same lock held). stop() in both bindings takes the same wrapper lock first, so a stream that delivers no data can deadlock: reader parks holding the lock, stop() blocks on the lock and never signals terminal.

Mitigations to evaluate:
1. Clone/hand out a read handle that outlives the lock (AudioCapture::read_chunk_blocking takes &self - the lock only exists to guard the Option<AudioCapture> wrapper slot, so a short-lived lock to grab a reference and a park OUTSIDE the lock may suffice - needs an Arc<AudioCapture> or equivalent restructure).
2. Split stop signaling out of the lifecycle mutex (e.g. expose request_stop() via an atomic/terminal signal that doesn't need the wrapper lock; core's request_stop(&self) exists for exactly this).
3. Bounded park with re-check (worse; papering over).

Affects: bindings/rsac-napi/src/lib.rs read_blocking + read_blocking_async + stop (:384), bindings/rsac-python/src/lib.rs read (:1027+) + stop. The core Rust API is NOT affected (read_chunk_blocking takes &self and request_stop unblocks it without any external mutex).

Origin: PR #54 CodeRabbit thread (Critical/heavy-lift); dispositioned valid-defer because the exposure predates the PR and restructuring binding lock architecture is not patch scope.

title: Re-enable registry publish workflows once credentials are configured
description:
The three registry publish workflows were disabled 2026-07-17 (gh workflow disable) for the v0.4.2 release because publishing credentials are not configured: gh secret list shows NO repo secrets (no CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN, no NPM_TOKEN), and the RSAC_RELEASE_APP_ID/RSAC_RELEASE_APP_PRIVATE_KEY App secrets for the automatic fan-out are also unverified/absent.

Disabled (re-enable with 'gh workflow enable <id>'):
- Release (crates.io) — id 263769186 — needs CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN
- Release npm — id 263827000 — needs NPM_TOKEN
- Release PyPI — id 263827001 — needs PyPI Trusted Publisher configured for the repo (OIDC, no secret)

Still active: Release Prepare + Release Tag on Merge (tag + GitHub Release flow works without registry creds).

To re-enable per registry: configure the credential, gh workflow enable <id>, then publish the backlog of unpublished tags via each workflow's workflow_dispatch (dry_run first, then publish:true with the version input). Note release.yml's semver-checks baseline expects consecutive stable tags — publishing 0.4.2 later from the tag ref is fine. Also note rsac-43e4 (first crates.io publish) depends on this.

title: AGENTS.md:406 — refresh Blacksmith Windows audio-probe evidence (2026-07-17 live re-probe)
description:
Live re-probe 2026-07-17 (run 29545897122, rootfs windows25-full-x64-251112) re-confirmed the core claim — AudioSrv/AudioEndpointBuilder absent, AudioSes.dll/AudioEndpointBuilder.dll/Audiodg.exe missing, MMDevices Render/Capture registry keys absent, no usable endpoint — but three details in AGENTS.md:406 need updating:

title: Compose: route 'Composition is not started' StreamReadError through a REASON_* constant so lifecycle_stage() classifies it
description: Follow-up from the rsac-feb4 adversarial review (F2, CONFIRMED/low): src/compose/stream.rs:855 constructs StreamReadError { reason: "Composition is not started. Call start() first." } without using a REASON_* constant, so AudioError::lifecycle_stage() returns LifecycleStage::Unknown for a genuine lifecycle cause — the one exception to the 'every lifecycle-cause StreamReadError classifies structurally' invariant established on fix/backlog-wave-1. Fix: route it through a shared constant (REASON_NOT_RUNNING or a compose-specific NotStarted stage). Not a regression; no test converts this site.
